title.zh: 卷四 孝和孝殤帝紀
title.en: Volume 4: Annals of Emperor Xiaohe; Emperor Xiaoshang
title.raw: 卷四 孝和孝殤帝紀 Volume 4: Annals of Emperor Xiaohe; Emperor Xiaoshang

sentences.zh: 孝和皇帝諱肇，肅宗第四子也。

translations.literal: The Filial and Harmonious Emperor, whose taboo name was Zhao, was the fourth son of Emperor Suzong (Zhang).
translations.idiomatic: Emperor He, whose personal name was Zhao, was the fourth son of Emperor Zhang (posthumously known as the "Filial" sovereign).

sentences.zh: 母梁貴人，為竇皇后所譖，憂卒，竇后養帝以為己子。

translations.literal: His mother was the Honored Lady Liang; she was slandered by Empress Dou and died of grief; Empress Dou raised the emperor as her own son.
translations.idiomatic: His mother was Lady Liang the Honored Lady. Empress Dou maligned her until she died of grief, and the empress then raised the boy as if he were her own child.

sentences.zh: 建初七年，立為皇太子。

translations.literal: In the seventh year of the Jianchu era, he was established as heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: In 82 CE (Jianchu 7), he was named crown prince.

sentences.zh: 章和二年二月壬辰，即皇帝位，年十歲。

translations.literal: On the renchen day of the second month in the second year of the Zhanghe era, he assumed the imperial throne; he was ten years old.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 March 88 CE (Zhanghe 2, day renchen of the second month), he ascended the throne at the age of ten.

sentences.zh: 尊皇后曰皇太后，太后臨朝。

translations.literal: The empress was honored as empress dowager; the empress dowager presided over court from behind the screen.
translations.idiomatic: The empress was elevated to empress dowager, who then ruled the court as regent.

sentences.zh: 三月丁酉，改淮陽為陳國，楚郡為彭城國，西平幷汝南郡，六安復為廬江郡。

translations.literal: On the dingyou day of the third month, Huaiyang was changed to the state of Chen, the Chu commandery to the state of Pengcheng, Xiping was merged into Runan commandery, and Luan was again made Lujiang commandery.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 April 88 CE (day dingyou of the third month), Huaiyang became the principality of Chen, the old Chu commandery became Pengcheng, Xiping was folded into Runan, and Luan was reorganized once more as Lujiang commandery.

sentences.zh: 遺詔徙西平王羨為陳王，六安王恭為彭城王。

translations.literal: By testamentary edict, King Xian of Xiping was transferred to be king of Chen, and King Gong of Luan to be king of Pengcheng.
translations.idiomatic: The late emperor's will moved King Xian of Xiping to Chen and King Gong of Luan to Pengcheng.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，葬孝章皇帝于敬陵。

translations.literal: On the guimao day, the Filial and Exemplary Emperor Zhang was buried at Jing Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 April 88 CE (guimao), Emperor Zhang was laid to rest at the Jing mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 庚戌，皇太后詔曰：『先帝以明聖，奉承祖宗至德要道，天下清靜，庶事咸寧。

translations.literal: On the gengxu day, the empress dowager issued an edict saying: 'The late emperor, by his enlightenment and sagacity, received and upheld the utmost virtue and essential way of the ancestors; the realm was tranquil and all affairs were at peace.
translations.idiomatic: On 17 April 88 CE (gengxu), the empress dowager proclaimed: "The late sovereign, enlightened and sage, carried forward the ancestors' supreme virtue and guiding principles, so the empire knew quiet and every matter knew rest."

sentences.zh: 今皇帝以幼年，煢煢在疚，朕且佐助聽政。

translations.literal: Now the emperor is in his minority, orphaned and in mourning; We for the time being shall assist and listen to government.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor is still a child, alone in mourning; I shall for now aid him and hear affairs of state."

sentences.zh: 外有大國賢王並為蕃屏，內有公卿大夫統理本朝，恭己受成，夫何憂哉！

translations.literal: Without there are great states and worthy kings who together are fence and screen; within there are dukes, ministers, and grandees who order and govern this court—reverently self-restrained and receiving what is accomplished—what is there to worry about!
translations.idiomatic: Abroad, great kingdoms and worthy princes stand as our bulwarks; at home, the high ministers order the dynasty. If we hold ourselves in reverent restraint and accept what is already settled, what cause is there for alarm?"

sentences.zh: 然守文之際，必有內輔以參聽斷。

translations.literal: Yet in a time of preserving the written legacy, there must be inner assistants to join in hearing and deciding.
translations.idiomatic: Even so, while we preserve the established pattern, we still need inner counsellors to share in deliberation and judgment."

sentences.zh: 侍中憲，朕之元兄，行能兼備，忠孝尤篤，先帝所器，親受遺詔，當以舊典輔斯職焉。

translations.literal: Palace Attendant Xian is Our eldest brother by birth; in conduct and ability he combines both; in loyalty and filial piety he is especially earnest; the late emperor esteemed him and he personally received the testamentary edict—according to precedent he ought to assist in this office.
translations.idiomatic: Palace Attendant Dou Xian is my eldest brother: his talent and character are complete, his loyalty and filial devotion profound. The late emperor valued him and entrusted him with the will; by established precedent he should fill this supporting role."

sentences.zh: 憲固執謙讓，節不可奪。

translations.literal: Xian firmly held to modest refusal; his integrity could not be wrested away.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian has stubbornly declined the honor, and his resolve cannot be shaken."

sentences.zh: 今供養兩宮，宿衛左右，厥事已重，亦不可復勞以政事。

translations.literal: Now in supplying the two palaces and standing night guard at left and right, the tasks are already heavy; moreover he cannot again be burdened with administrative affairs.
translations.idiomatic: He already bears the heavy duty of attending the two palaces and guarding the throne; he cannot be loaded further with routine government."

sentences.zh: 故太尉鄧彪，元功之族，三讓彌高，海內歸仁，為群賢首，先帝褒表，欲以崇化。

translations.literal: The former Grand Commandant Deng Biao, of a clan of prime merit, thrice yielded and his repute mounted ever higher; within the seas men turned to his benevolence; he was chief among the host of worthies; the late emperor praised and displayed him, wishing thereby to exalt transformation.
translations.idiomatic: The former Grand Commandant Deng Biao springs from a family of the founding merit; thrice he yielded office and his fame only rose. The realm looked to his humanity as to the foremost of the worthy. The late emperor singled him out in order to exalt moral example."

sentences.zh: 今彪聰明康強，可謂老成黃耇矣。

translations.literal: Now Biao is perceptive, strong, and hale—he may be called mature and hoary-headed.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Biao remains clear-minded and vigorous—the very model of a seasoned elder statesman."

sentences.zh: 其以彪為太傅，賜爵關內侯，錄尚書事，百官總己以聽，朕庶幾得專心內位。

translations.literal: Let Biao be made Grand Tutor, enfeoffed with rank as marquis within the passes, and put in charge of the Masters of Writing; the hundred officials shall gather themselves and obey; We may thereby hope to give Our mind solely to inner duties.
translations.idiomatic: Appoint him Grand Tutor, grant him the title of marquis within the passes, and charge him with the Masters of Writing. Let every official answer to him; thus I may hope to devote myself to domestic concerns."

sentences.zh: 於戲！

translations.literal: Oh!
translations.idiomatic: Alas!"

sentences.zh: 群公其勉率百僚，各修厥職，愛養元元，綏以中和，稱朕意焉。』

translations.literal: You lords, strive to lead the hundred officials; each cultivate his office; cherish and nurture the black-haired people; pacify them with centrality and harmony—thus accord with Our intent.'
translations.idiomatic: Let you gentlemen urge the bureaucracy onward: each man to his duty, cherishing the common people and settling them in even-tempered harmony—that will match my wishes."

sentences.zh: 辛酉，有司上奏：『孝章皇帝崇弘鴻業，德化普洽，垂意黎民，留念稼穡。

translations.literal: On the xinyou day, the responsible officials submitted a memorial saying: 'The Filial and Exalted Emperor Zhang exalted and enlarged the great enterprise; his virtuous transformation spread everywhere; he extended his mind to the black-haired people and kept in mind sowing and reaping.
translations.idiomatic: On 24 April 88 CE (xinyou), officials memorialized: "Emperor Zhang magnified the great work of state; his moral influence reached everywhere; he turned his care to the people and never forgot the plough and the harvest."

sentences.zh: 文加殊俗，武暢方表，界惟人面，無思不服。

translations.literal: Civil virtue extended to alien customs; martial prowess penetrated the regions beyond; within the bounds only human faces—none whose thoughts did not submit.
translations.idiomatic: His civil sway reached distant peoples; his military power swept the frontiers. Within the known world every face was human, and none withheld allegiance."

sentences.zh: 巍巍蕩蕩，莫與比隆。

translations.literal: Lofty, vast—none with whom to compare his elevation.
translations.idiomatic: So towering and boundless was his reign that none could rival its height."

sentences.zh: 《周頌》曰：「於穆清廟，肅雝顯相。

translations.literal: The 'Zhou Hymns' says: 'Oh, solemn is the clear temple; reverent and harmonious are the illustrious assistants.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhou hymns run: "How august the bright ancestral temple—how grave and harmonious its shining ministers."

sentences.zh: 」請上尊廟曰肅宗，共進《武德》之舞。

translations.literal: ' They requested that the exalted temple title be raised to Suzong and that together they advance the dance of 'Martial Virtue.'
translations.idiomatic: " They asked that his temple name be elevated to Suzong and that the "Martial Virtue" dance be performed at his sacrifices."

sentences.zh: 』制曰：『可。

translations.literal: ' The rescript said: 'Approved.'
translations.idiomatic: " The edict replied: "Granted."

sentences.zh: 』癸亥，陳王羨、彭城王恭、樂成王黨、下邳王衍、梁王暢始就國。

translations.literal: ' On the guihai day, the kings of Chen Xian, Pengcheng Gong, Lecheng Dang, Xiapi Yan, and Liang Chang for the first time went to their states.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 April 88 CE (guihai), Kings Xian of Chen, Gong of Pengcheng, Dang of Lecheng, Yan of Xiapi, and Chang of Liang departed for their fiefs.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙子，謁高廟。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, on the bingzi day, he visited Gao's temple.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer, on 8 May 88 CE (bingzi), he paid homage at the temple of Emperor Gaozu.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，謁世祖廟。

translations.literal: On the dingchou day, he visited Shizu's temple.
translations.idiomatic: On the following day (dingchou), he visited the temple of Emperor Guangwu (Shizu).

sentences.zh: 戊寅，詔曰：『昔孝武皇帝致誅胡、越，故權收鹽鐵之利，以奉師旅之費。

translations.literal: On the wuyin day an edict said: 'In the past the Filial Martial Emperor carried out punishment against the Hu and Yue; therefore he temporarily gathered the profits from salt and iron to supply the expenses of armies.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 May 88 CE (wuyin) an edict ran: "Long ago Emperor Wu campaigned against the northern tribes and the Yue; for a time he monopolized salt and iron revenues to pay for the armies."

sentences.zh: 自中興以來，匈奴未賓，永平末年，復修征伐。

translations.literal: Since the restoration, the Xiongnu have not been guests; in the last years of the Yongping era, campaigns were again undertaken.
translations.idiomatic: After the restoration the Xiongnu had not truly submitted, and in the closing years of Yongping the court again took up the sword."

sentences.zh: 先帝即位，務休力役，然猶深思遠慮，安不忘危，探觀舊典，復收鹽鐵，欲以防備不虞，寧安邊境。

translations.literal: When the late emperor ascended the throne, he strove to rest corvée labor; yet still he thought deeply and planned far, in security not forgetting peril; examining the old statutes, he again gathered salt and iron, wishing thereby to guard against the unforeseen and pacify and secure the borders.
translations.idiomatic: The late Emperor Zhang sought to ease the people's burdens, yet he never forgot danger in times of peace. Reviewing precedent, he restored the salt-and-iron monopoly to hedge against surprise and to steady the frontier."

sentences.zh: 而吏多不良，動失其便，以違上意。

translations.literal: But the clerks were mostly not good; in their movements they missed what was advantageous, thereby contravening superiors' intent.
translations.idiomatic: Too many local officials proved incompetent; their handling constantly missed the mark and frustrated the throne's purpose."

sentences.zh: 先帝恨之，故遺戒郡國罷鹽鐵之禁，縱民煮鑄，入稅縣官如故事。

translations.literal: The late emperor resented this; therefore in his testamentary admonition he ordered the commanderies and kingdoms to abolish the prohibitions on salt and iron, allowing the people to boil and cast, paying tax to the county official as in former practice.
translations.idiomatic: He bitterly regretted that, and in his final instructions he told every commandery and kingdom to lift the salt-and-iron restrictions, let the people produce for themselves, and pay tax to the government as of old."

sentences.zh: 其申勑刺史、二千石，奉順聖旨，勉弘德化，布告天下，使明知朕意。』

translations.literal: Let there be reiterated orders to the regional inspectors and two-thousand-dan officials: obey and comply with the sage decree; strive to enlarge virtuous transformation; proclaim it throughout the realm, causing all clearly to know Our intent.'
translations.idiomatic: Let the regional inspectors and commandery governors be charged anew to heed this sacred command, promote humane government, and publish it abroad so that everyone understands my meaning."

sentences.zh: 五月，京師旱。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, the capital region suffered drought.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month drought struck the capital.

sentences.zh: 詔長樂少府桓郁侍講禁中。

translations.literal: An edict ordered the Superintendent of Changle Huan Yu to attend lecturing within the forbidden precincts.
translations.idiomatic: The court ordered Huan Yu, Superintendent of the Changle palace, to give lectures inside the palace.

sentences.zh: 冬十月乙亥，以侍中竇憲為車騎將軍，伐北匈奴。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, on the yihai day, Palace Attendant Dou Xian was made General of Chariots and Cavalry to attack the Northern Xiongnu.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter, on 17 November 88 CE (yihai), Dou Xian was named General of Chariots and Cavalry and sent against the Northern Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 安息國遣使獻師子、扶拔。

translations.literal: The state of Anxi sent envoys presenting a lion and fuba.
translations.idiomatic: Parthia sent tribute-bearers with a lion and exotic beasts (fuba).

sentences.zh: 永元元年春三月甲辰，初令郎官詔除者得占丞、尉，以比秩為真。

translations.literal: In spring, the third month of the first year of the Yongyuan era, on the jiachen day, it was first ordered that Gentlemen of the Palace appointed by edict might hold assistant magistrate and sheriff posts, with comparable rank made substantive.
translations.idiomatic: On 14 April 89 CE (Yongyuan 1, third month, jiachen), for the first time Gentlemen appointed by imperial rescript were allowed to take assistant and sheriff posts at equivalent rank as regular appointments.

sentences.zh: 夏六月，車騎將軍竇憲出雞鹿塞，度遼將軍鄧鴻出稒陽塞，南單于出滿夷谷，與北匈奴戰於稽落山，大破之，追至私渠比鞮海。

translations.literal: In summer, the sixth month, General of Chariots and Cavalry Dou Xian went out by Jimu Pass, General Who Crosses the Liao Deng Hong by Guyang Pass, and the Southern Chanyu by Manyi Valley; they fought the Northern Xiongnu at Mount Jiluo, greatly defeated them, and pursued to the Siqu Bidihai.
translations.idiomatic: That summer Dou Xian marched through Jimu Pass, Deng Hong Who Crosses the Liao through Guyang, and the Southern Chanyu through Manyi Valley. They crushed the Northern Xiongnu at Mount Jiluo and chased them to Lake Siqu Bidihi.

sentences.zh: 竇憲遂登燕然山，刻石勒功而還。

translations.literal: Dou Xian then ascended Mount Yanran, carved stone to inscribe the merit, and returned.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian climbed Mount Yanran, had the victory carved in stone—the famous "Yanran inscription"—and withdrew.

sentences.zh: 北單于遣弟右溫禺鞮王奉奏貢獻。

translations.literal: The Northern Chanyu sent his younger brother, the king of the Right Wenyuqi, to present a memorial and tribute.
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Chanyu sent his brother, the Prince of the Right Wenyuqi, with a petition and gifts of submission.

sentences.zh: 秋七月乙未，會稽山崩。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, on the yiwei day, a mountain in Kuaiji collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn, on 21 August 89 CE (yiwei), a landslide struck Mount Kuaiji.

sentences.zh: 閏月丙子，詔曰：『匈奴背叛，為害久遠。

translations.literal: Intercalary month, bingzi day, an edict said: 'The Xiongnu rebelled and betrayed; the harm has been long-standing.
translations.idiomatic: On 20 September 89 CE (intercalary seventh month, bingzi) an edict declared: "The Xiongnu have long rebelled and done harm."

sentences.zh: 賴祖宗之靈，師克有捷，醜虜破碎，遂掃厥庭，役不再籍，萬里清蕩，非朕小子眇身所能克堪。

translations.literal: Relying on the numen of the ancestors, the army was able to have victory; the ugly captives were smashed; then We swept their court; corvée was not again registered; for ten thousand li all was cleared and purified—not something this small person of Ours, the boy, could have borne.
translations.idiomatic: Thanks to the spirits of our forebears the host prevailed: the foe shattered, their stronghold swept clean, without a second levy of troops, peace restored across a thousand miles—no achievement my slight person could have sustained alone."

sentences.zh: 有司其案舊典，告類薦功，以章休烈。』

translations.literal: Let the responsible officials investigate the old statutes, report the category, present the merit, thereby to manifest the splendid merit.'
translations.idiomatic: Let the officials consult precedent, report the victory to Heaven, and offer thanks for the triumph, that this glorious deed may be known."

sentences.zh: 九月庚申，以車騎將軍竇憲為大將軍，以中郎將劉尚為車騎將軍。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, on the gengshen day, General of Chariots and Cavalry Dou Xian was made Grand General; General of the Gentlemen of the Household Liu Shang was made General of Chariots and Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 October 89 CE (ninth month, gengshen), Dou Xian was promoted to Grand General and Liu Shang, a general of the household, succeeded him as General of Chariots and Cavalry.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，令郡國刑輸作軍營，其徙出塞者，刑雖未竟，皆免歸田里。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, it was ordered that in the commanderies and kingdoms convicts laboring in military camps—those transferred beyond the passes—even if their sentence was not yet finished, all were released to return to fields and hamlets.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter the court ordered convicts sent to frontier camps—and even those exiled beyond the passes whose terms were unfinished—to be freed and sent home to farm.

sentences.zh: 庚子，阜陵王延薨。

translations.literal: On the gengzi day, King Yan of Fuling died.
translations.idiomatic: On 13 November 89 CE (gengzi), King Yan of Fuling died.

sentences.zh: 是歲，郡國九大水。

translations.literal: That year, nine commanderies and kingdoms suffered great floods.
translations.idiomatic: That year nine commanderies and kingdoms were hit by severe flooding.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月丁丑，大赦天下。

translations.literal: Second year, spring, first month, dingchou day, a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On 31 January 90 CE (Yongyuan 2, first month, dingchou), the empire received a general amnesty.

sentences.zh: 二月壬午，日有食之。

translations.literal: Second month, renwu day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse occurred on 5 February 90 CE (renwu).

sentences.zh: 己亥，復置西河、上郡屬國都尉官。

translations.literal: On the jihai day, the dependent-state commandant posts for Xihe and Shang commandery were re-established.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 February 90 CE (jihai), the dependent-state commandant offices for Xihe and Shang were restored.

sentences.zh: 夏五月庚戌，分太山為濟北國，分樂成、涿郡、勃海為河間國。

translations.literal: In summer, the fifth month, on the gengxu day, Mount Tai was divided to make the state of Jibei; Lecheng, Zhuo commandery, and Bohai were divided to make the state of Hejian.
translations.idiomatic: On 2 June 90 CE (fifth month, gengxu), territory was carved from Taishan commandery to form the principality of Jibei, and from Lecheng, Zhuo, and Bohai to form Hejian.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，封皇弟壽為濟北王，開為河間王，淑為城陽王，紹封故淮陽王昞子側為常山王。

translations.literal: On the bingchen day, the emperor's younger brothers Shou, Kai, and Shu were enfeoffed as kings of Jibei, Hejian, and Chengyang respectively; continuing the fief, the son Ce of the former king Bing of Huaiyang was enfeoffed as king of Changshan.
translations.idiomatic: On 8 June 90 CE (bingchen), the emperor's brothers Shou, Kai, and Shu became princes of Jibei, Hejian, and Chengyang respectively, and Ce, son of the late King Bing of Huaiyang, was reinvested as prince of Changshan.

sentences.zh: 賜公卿以下至佐史錢、布各有差。

translations.literal: From dukes and ministers down to assistant clerks, cash and cloth were bestowed, each according to rank.
translations.idiomatic: Cash and cloth were distributed on a graded scale from the highest ministers down to clerical aides.

sentences.zh: 己未，遣副校尉閻磐討北匈奴，取伊吾盧地。

translations.literal: On the jiwei day, Deputy Colonel Yan Pan was sent to punish the Northern Xiongnu and take the land of Yiwulu.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 June 90 CE (jiwei), Deputy Colonel Yan Pan was dispatched against the Northern Xiongnu and seized the Yiwu region.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，紹封故齊王晃子無忌為齊王，北海王睦子威為北海王。

translations.literal: On the dingmao day, continuing the fief, the son Wuji of the former king Huang of Qi was enfeoffed as king of Qi; the son Wei of King Mu of Beihai was enfeoffed as king of Beihai.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 June 90 CE (dingmao), Wuji, son of the late Prince Huang of Qi, was restored to Qi, and Wei, son of Prince Mu of Beihai, succeeded his father in Beihai.

sentences.zh: 車師前後王並遣子入侍。

translations.literal: The kings of Front and Rear Jushi both sent sons to attend at court.
translations.idiomatic: The rulers of both divisions of Jushi sent hostages—their sons—to the Han court.

sentences.zh: 月氏國遣兵攻西域長史班超，超擊降之。

translations.literal: The state of the Yuezhi sent troops to attack the Chief Clerk of the Western Regions Ban Chao; Chao struck and made them surrender.
translations.idiomatic: The Yuezhi sent an army against Ban Chao, Chief Clerk of the Western Regions; he defeated them and forced their submission.

sentences.zh: 六月辛卯，中山王焉薨。

translations.literal: Sixth month, xinmao day, King Yan of Zhongshan died.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 July 90 CE (sixth month, xinmao), King Yan of Zhongshan died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月乙卯，大將軍竇憲出屯涼州。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, yimao day, Grand General Dou Xian went out and encamped at Liangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn, on 15 August 90 CE (seventh month, yimao), Grand General Dou Xian marched to garrison Liangzhou.

sentences.zh: 九月，北匈奴遣使稱臣。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, the Northern Xiongnu sent envoys declaring themselves subjects.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month the Northern Chanyu sent envoys offering submission as a vassal.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，遣行中郎將班固報命南單于。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, Acting General of the Gentlemen of the Household Ban Gu was sent to return the mandate to the Southern Chanyu.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter Ban Gu, acting general of the household, was sent to convey the imperial response to the Southern Chanyu.

sentences.zh: 遣左谷蠡王師子出雞鹿塞，擊北匈奴於河雲北，大破之。

translations.literal: The Left Guili King Shizi was sent out Jimu Pass to strike the Northern Xiongnu north of Heyun; he greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: The Southern Chanyu's Left Guili King, Shizi, was sent through Jimu Pass and routed the Northern Xiongnu north of the Heyun River.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月甲子，皇帝加元服，賜諸侯王、公、將軍、特進、中二千石、列侯、宗室子孫在京師奉朝請者黃金，將、大夫、郎吏、從官帛。

translations.literal: Third year, spring, first month, jiazi day, the emperor added the cap-of-adulthood; to the kings of the feudal states, dukes, generals, specially advanced, full two-thousand-dan salary officials, marquises, and imperial-clan descendants in the capital attending court audience, gold was bestowed; to generals, grandees, Gentlemen, clerks, and attendants, silk.
translations.idiomatic: On 16 February 91 CE (Yongyuan 3, first month, jiazi), the emperor came of age. Gold went to princes, dukes, generals, officials of specially advanced and full two-thousand-dan rank, marquises, and imperial kinsmen on court duty in the capital; silk to generals, senior officials, Gentlemen, clerks, and attendants.

sentences.zh: 賜民爵及粟帛各有差，大酺五日。

translations.literal: The people were granted noble ranks and grain and silk, each according to gradation; a great feast for five days.
translations.idiomatic: Commoners received ranked noble titles, grain, and cloth on a sliding scale, and the capital enjoyed five days of public feasting.

sentences.zh: 郡國中都官繫囚死罪贖縑，至司寇及亡命，各有差。

translations.literal: In commanderies, kingdoms, and offices under central jurisdiction, imprisoned convicts: death sentences might be commuted by silk ransom, down to Sikou penal labor and fugitives—each category according to differentiated rules.
translations.idiomatic: Across commanderies, kingdoms, and capital prisons, convicts could buy remission with silk on a sliding scale from capital cases through penal servitude (Sikou) to fugitives under warrant.

sentences.zh: 庚辰，賜京師民酺，布兩戶共一匹。

translations.literal: On the gengchen day, a feast was granted to the capital people; cloth, two households sharing one bolt.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 February 91 CE (gengchen) the people of the capital were given a feast-day and one bolt of cloth for every two households.

sentences.zh: 二月，大將軍竇憲遣左校尉耿夔出居延塞，圍北單于於金微山，大破之，獲其母閼氏。

translations.literal: Second month, Grand General Dou Xian sent Colonel of the Left Geng Kui out by the Juyan Pass to surround the Northern Chanyu at Mount Jinwei; he greatly defeated him and captured his mother the Yanzhi.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month Grand General Dou Xian ordered Colonel Geng Kui through Juyan Pass to trap the Northern Chanyu at Mount Jinwei. The Han army shattered the Xiongnu host and took the chanyu's queen mother captive.

sentences.zh: 夏六月辛卯，尊皇太後母比陽公主為長公主。

translations.literal: Summer, sixth month, xinmao day, the empress dowager's mother, the princess of Biyang, was honored as senior princess.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 July 91 CE (sixth month, xinmao), the empress dowager's mother, the Princess of Biyang, was elevated to senior princess.

sentences.zh: 辛丑，阜陵王種薨。

translations.literal: On the xinchou day, King Zhong of Fuling died.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 August 91 CE (xinchou), King Zhong of Fuling died.

sentences.zh: 冬十月癸未，行幸長安。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, guiwei day, he traveled and favored Chang'an with his presence.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter, on 6 November 91 CE (guiwei), the emperor journeyed to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『北狄破滅，名王仍降，西域諸國，納質內附，豈非祖宗迪哲重光之鴻烈歟？

translations.literal: An edict said: 'The northern Di have been destroyed and broken; famous kings repeatedly surrender; the various states of the Western Regions send hostages and attach inward—is this not the vast merit of the ancestors' guiding wisdom and redoubled radiance?
translations.idiomatic: The edict continued: the northern tribes are shattered, their great chiefs keep surrendering, and the Western Regions send hostages and pledge allegiance—surely this is the splendid achievement of wise forebears and renewed glory.

sentences.zh: 寤寐嘆息，想望舊京。

translations.literal: Waking and sleeping I sigh, longing for the old capital.
translations.idiomatic: Night and day I sigh, yearning for the ancient capital.

sentences.zh: 其賜行所過二千石長吏已下及三老、官屬錢帛，各有差；

translations.literal: Let there be bestowed along the route of travel to two-thousand-dan senior officials and below, and the three elders and official subordinates, cash and silk, each according to gradation;
translations.idiomatic: Let cash and silk be granted along my route to local magistrates of two-thousand-dan rank and below, to the village elders, and to their staffs—each according to station;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人三斛。』

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the seriously ill and crippled, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain, three hu per person.'
translations.idiomatic: and to widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the gravely ill, and the destitute, three hu of grain apiece.

sentences.zh: 十一月癸卯，祠高廟，遂有事十一陵。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, guimao day, he sacrificed at Gao's temple; then he performed the rites at the eleven mausolea.
translations.idiomatic: On 25 November 91 CE (eleventh month, guimao), he offered sacrifice at Gaozu's temple and then at the eleven imperial tombs in turn.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『高祖功臣，蕭、曹為首，有傳世不絕之義。

translations.literal: An edict said: 'Among the merit subjects of Gaozu, Xiao and Cao were chief; there is the principle of transmission through generations without break.
translations.idiomatic: The edict went on: among Gaozu's founding ministers, Xiao He and Cao Shen stood first, and their houses were meant to pass down without fail.

sentences.zh: 曹相國後容城侯無嗣。

translations.literal: Among the posterity of Chancellor Cao, the marquis of Rongcheng has no heir.
translations.idiomatic: Yet Cao Shen's descendant, the Marquis of Rongcheng, has left no successor.

sentences.zh: 朕望長陵東門，見二臣之壠，循其遠節，每有感焉。

translations.literal: We gaze from the east gate of Changling and see the mounds of the two ministers; following their remote integrity, each time there is feeling.
translations.idiomatic: When I look from the east gate of Gaozu's tomb toward the barrows of those two ministers, their steadfast virtue moves me every time.

sentences.zh: 忠義獲寵，古今所同。

translations.literal: Loyalty and righteousness receiving favor is the same in ancient and modern.
translations.idiomatic: Loyal service has always earned the throne's favor—past and present agree on that.

sentences.zh: 可遣使者以中牢祠，大鴻臚求近親宜為嗣者，須景風紹封，以章厥功。』

translations.literal: It is permitted to send envoys to sacrifice with middle victims; let the Grand Herald seek near kin suitable to be heirs; wait for the Jingfeng wind to renew the fief, thereby to manifest their merit.'
translations.idiomatic: Send envoys with a medium sacrifice, and let the Grand Herald find close kin fit to inherit; when the summer "Jingfeng" season comes, renew the fief and so honor their achievement.

sentences.zh: 十二月，復置西域都護、騎都尉、戊己校尉官。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, the Protector-General of the Western Regions, Colonel of Cavalry, and Colonel of the Gengji garrison posts were re-established.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month the court restored the Protector-General of the Western Regions, the Colonel of Cavalry, and the Wuji (Gengji) colonels.

sentences.zh: 庚辰，至自長安，減刑徒從駕者刑五月。

translations.literal: On the gengchen day, having arrived from Chang'an, the sentences of convict laborers who had accompanied the carriage were reduced by five months.
translations.idiomatic: On 10 December 91 CE (gengchen), returning from Chang'an, he shortened by five months the terms of convicts who had served on the imperial retinue.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月，北匈奴右谷蠡王於除鞬自立為單于，款塞乞降。

translations.literal: Fourth year, spring, first month, the Northern Xiongnu Right Guili King Yuchujian set himself up as chanyu, knocked at the passes, and begged to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of spring, 92 CE (Yongyuan 4), the Northern Xiongnu's Right Guili King, Yuchujian, declared himself chanyu and came to the frontier seeking submission.

sentences.zh: 遣大將軍左校尉耿夔授璽綬。

translations.literal: Grand General's Colonel of the Left Geng Kui was sent to confer the seal and ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Geng Kui of the Grand General's staff was dispatched to invest him with the chanyu's seal and cord.

sentences.zh: 三月癸丑，司徒袁安薨。

translations.literal: Third month, guichou day, Minister of Education Yuan An died.
translations.idiomatic: On 29 April 92 CE (third month, guichou), Minister of Education Yuan An died.

sentences.zh: 閏月丁丑，太常丁鴻為司徒。

translations.literal: Intercalary month, dingchou day, Grand Master of Ceremonies Ding Hong became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On 18 May 92 CE (intercalary third month, dingchou), Ding Hong, Grand Master of Ceremonies, was appointed Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙辰，大將軍竇憲還至京師。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, bingchen day, Grand General Dou Xian returned and arrived at the capital.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 June 92 CE (fourth month, bingchen), Grand General Dou Xian returned to the capital.

sentences.zh: 六月戊戌朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: Sixth month, wuxu new-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse marked the new moon of the sixth month, 23 July 92 CE (wuxu).

sentences.zh: 丙辰，郡國十三地震。

translations.literal: On the bingchen day, thirteen commanderies and kingdoms had earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: On 10 August 92 CE (bingchen), earthquakes struck thirteen commanderies and kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 竇憲潛圖弒逆。

translations.literal: Dou Xian secretly plotted regicide and rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian was discovered plotting to murder the emperor and usurp the throne.

sentences.zh: 庚申，幸北宮。

translations.literal: On the gengshen day, he favored the Northern Palace with his presence.
translations.idiomatic: On 14 August 92 CE (gengshen), the emperor took up residence in the Northern Palace.

sentences.zh: 詔收捕憲黨射聲校尉郭璜，璜子侍中舉，衛尉鄧疊，疊弟步兵校尉磊。

translations.literal: An edict ordered the arrest of Dou Xian's faction: Colonel of the Archers Who Shoot at a Sound Guo Huang, Huang's son the Palace Attendant Ju, Commandant of the Guards Deng Die, and Die's younger brother Colonel of the Foot Soldiers Lei.
translations.idiomatic: By edict the court seized Dou Xian's allies: Colonel Guo Huang of the Archers Who Shoot by Sound, his son Palace Attendant Guo Ju, Commandant of the Guards Deng Die, and Deng Die's brother Colonel Deng Lei.

sentences.zh: 皆下獄死。

translations.literal: All were cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: All were imprisoned and put to death.

sentences.zh: 使謁者僕射收憲大將軍印綬，遣憲及弟篤、景就國，到皆自殺。

translations.literal: The Supervisor of the Imperial Messengers was sent to take Dou Xian's Grand General seal and ribbon; Xian and his younger brothers Du and Jing were sent to their states; on arrival all committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: The Supervisor of Messengers stripped Dou Xian of his Grand General's insignia and banished him with his brothers Dou Du and Dou Jing to their fiefs; all three killed themselves on arrival.

sentences.zh: 是夏、旱、蝗。

translations.literal: That summer there was drought and locusts.
translations.idiomatic: That summer brought drought and locust swarms.

sentences.zh: 秋七月己丑，太尉宋由坐黨憲自殺。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, jichou day, Grand Commandant Song You, sitting in faction with Dou Xian, committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: On 27 August 92 CE (seventh month, jichou), Grand Commandant Song You killed himself after being implicated as Dou Xian's partisan.

sentences.zh: 八月辛亥，司空任隗薨。

translations.literal: Eighth month, xinhai day, Minister of Works Ren Kui died.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 September 92 CE, eighth month, day xinhai, Minister of Works Ren Kui died.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，大司農尹睦為太尉，錄尚書事。

translations.literal: On the guichou day, Grand Minister of Agriculture Yin Mu became Grand Commandant and was put in charge of the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: On 7 September 92 CE, Yin Mu rose from Grand Minister of Agriculture to Grand Commandant with authority over the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，賜公卿以下至佐史錢、穀各有差。

translations.literal: On the dingsi day, from dukes and ministers down to assistant clerks, cash and grain were bestowed, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 September 92 CE, officials from the highest ministers down to clerical aides received graded gifts of cash and grain.

sentences.zh: 冬十月己亥，宗正劉方為司空。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, jihai day, Director of the Imperial Clan Liu Fang became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 October 92 CE, Liu Fang, Director of the Imperial Clan, was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 十二月壬辰，詔：『今年郡國秋稼為旱、蝗所傷，其什四以上勿收田租、芻稾；

translations.literal: Twelfth month, renchen day, an edict: 'This year in the commanderies and kingdoms the autumn crops were harmed by drought and locusts; where damage is forty percent or more, do not collect field rent or fodder straw;
translations.idiomatic: On 26 November 92 CE, day renchen of the twelfth month, an edict ordered that wherever drought and locusts had destroyed forty percent or more of the autumn harvest, field tax and fodder levies were waived.

sentences.zh: 有不滿者，以實除之。

translations.literal: where it does not reach that, remove according to the actual amount.
translations.idiomatic: Where losses fell short of that threshold, remissions were to match the real damage.

sentences.zh: 』武陵零陵澧中蠻叛。

translations.literal: ' The Man of Wuling, Lingling, and Lizhong rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: The Man tribes of Wuling, Lingling, and the Li valley rose in revolt.

sentences.zh: 燒當羌寇金城。

translations.literal: The Shaodang Qiang raided Jincheng.
translations.idiomatic: The Shaodang Qiang plundered Jincheng commandery.

sentences.zh: 五年春正月乙亥，宗祀五帝於明堂，遂登靈臺，望雲物。

translations.literal: Fifth year, spring, first month, yihai day, he performed lineage sacrifice to the Five Thearchs in the Bright Hall; then he ascended the Spirit Terrace and observed clouds and prodigies.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 February 93 CE, Yongyuan 5, he sacrificed to the Five Thearchs in the Bright Hall, then climbed the Spirit Terrace to read the omens in sky and weather.

sentences.zh: 大赦天下。

translations.literal: A general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty was proclaimed throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 戊子，千乘王伉薨。

translations.literal: On the wuzi day, King Kang of Qiansheng died.
translations.idiomatic: On 24 February 93 CE, King Kang of Qiansheng died.

sentences.zh: 辛卯，封皇帝萬歲為廣宗王。

translations.literal: On the xinmao day, the emperor's son Wansui was enfeoffed as king of Guangzong.
translations.idiomatic: On 27 February 93 CE, the emperor's son Liu Wansui was made prince of Guangzong.

sentences.zh: 二月戊戌，詔有司省減內外廄及涼州諸苑馬。

translations.literal: Second month, wuxu day, it was ordered that the responsible officials reduce horses in the inner and outer stables and in the various parks of Liangzhou.
translations.idiomatic: On 7 March 93 CE, the court ordered cuts to the imperial stud and to horse herds kept in Liangzhou's hunting parks.

sentences.zh: 自京師離宮果園上林廣成囿悉以假貧民，恣得采捕，不收其稅。

translations.literal: From the capital's detached palaces, fruit orchards, Shanglin, and Guangcheng park, all were lent to poor commoners; they were allowed to gather and hunt freely; their tax was not collected.
translations.idiomatic: Detached palaces near the capital, their orchards, Shanglin Park, and the Guangcheng preserve were thrown open to the poor for gathering and fishing without tax.

sentences.zh: 丁未，詔曰：『去年秋麥入少，恐民食不足。

translations.literal: On the dingwei day an edict said: 'Last year's autumn wheat intake was small; We fear the people's food is insufficient.
translations.idiomatic: On 16 March 93 CE an edict noted that last autumn's wheat harvest had been thin and the people might lack food.

sentences.zh: 其上尤貧不能自給者戶口人數。

translations.literal: Let them report upward the household and mouth counts of those especially poor who cannot supply themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Commanderies were to report the number of households and persons among the destitute who could not feed themselves.

sentences.zh: 往者郡國上貧民，以衣履釜鬵為貲，而豪右得其饒利。

translations.literal: In the past when commanderies and kingdoms reported poor commoners, they took clothing, shoes, cauldrons, and pots as assets, while the powerful on the right got the surplus profit.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly, when counties listed the needy, corrupt clerks counted clothes and cooking pots as wealth, letting local magnates pocket the real gains.

sentences.zh: 詔書實覈，欲有以益之，而長吏不能躬親，反更徵召會聚，令失農作，愁擾百姓。

translations.literal: The edict ordered true verification, wishing thereby to benefit them; yet senior clerks could not personally attend, and on the contrary further summoned and assembled them, causing loss of agricultural work and vexing and disturbing the hundred surnames.
translations.idiomatic: The edict demanded honest registers so aid could reach the poor, yet magistrates often failed to go in person and instead rounded people up for pointless assemblies, ruining the farming season and harassing the populace.

sentences.zh: 若復有犯者，二千石先坐。』

translations.literal: If again there are offenders, let the two-thousand-dan officials bear guilt first.'
translations.idiomatic: Any repeat of such abuse would be punished first against the responsible two-thousand-dan governor.

sentences.zh: 甲寅，太傅鄧彪薨。

translations.literal: On the jiayin day, Grand Tutor Deng Biao died.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 March 93 CE, Grand Tutor Deng Biao died.

sentences.zh: 戊午，隴西地震。

translations.literal: On the wuwu day, Longxi had an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On 27 March 93 CE an earthquake struck Longxi commandery.

sentences.zh: 三月戊子，詔曰：『選舉良才，為政之本。

translations.literal: Third month, wuzi day, an edict said: 'Selecting and raising good talent is the root of governing.
translations.idiomatic: On 24 April 93 CE an edict declared that choosing able men for office lies at the heart of good government.

sentences.zh: 科別行能，必由鄉曲。

translations.literal: Sorting conduct and ability must come from village and ward.
translations.idiomatic: Assessing character and competence must begin in the villages and hamlets where candidates are known.

sentences.zh: 而郡國舉吏，不加簡擇，故先帝明勑在所，令試之以職，乃得充選。

translations.literal: Yet when commanderies and kingdoms recommend clerks, they do not add selection and choice; therefore the late emperor clearly ordered the places to test them in office before they may fill selection.
translations.idiomatic: Yet commanderies often nominated men without scrutiny, so the late emperor had ordered them to prove fitness in actual posts before confirmation.

sentences.zh: 又德行尤異，不須經職者，別署狀上。

translations.literal: Moreover, where conduct and virtue are especially unusual and they need not pass through office, separately sign the document and submit upward.
translations.idiomatic: Men of truly outstanding virtue who skipped probation were to be reported on separate memorials.

sentences.zh: 而宣布以來，出入九年，二千石曾不承奉，恣心從好，司隸、刺史訖無糾察。

translations.literal: Yet since proclamation, going on nine years, the two-thousand-dan have never obeyed; they follow their hearts according to preference; the Metropolitan Superintendent and regional inspectors to the end have had no investigation and inspection.
translations.idiomatic: Nine years after that decree, governors still ignored it, indulging private favor, while the Metropolitan Superintendent and regional inspectors failed to investigate.

sentences.zh: 今新蒙赦令，且復申勑，後有犯者，顯明其罰。

translations.literal: Now newly having received an amnesty edict, for the time being again issue reiterated orders; afterward, where there are offenders, clearly manifest their punishment.
translations.idiomatic: With a fresh amnesty now proclaimed, the court repeated its instructions: further violations would meet clear and public penalties.

sentences.zh: 在位不以選舉為憂，督察不以發覺為負，非獨州郡也。

translations.literal: Those in office do not take selection as a worry; those who supervise and inspect do not take discovery as a burden—not only the provinces and commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Those in high place showed no concern for honest nomination, and overseers treated failure to catch abuse as no fault of theirs—and the rot was not confined to the provinces.

sentences.zh: 是以庶官多非其人。

translations.literal: Therefore the multitude of offices mostly do not have the right men.
translations.idiomatic: Hence many offices were filled with the wrong men.

sentences.zh: 下民被姦邪之傷，由法不行故也。

translations.literal: The lower people receive injury from crafty evil—it is because the laws are not carried out.
translations.idiomatic: The common people suffered from knavery because the law was not enforced.

sentences.zh: 』庚寅，遣使者分行貧民，舉實流冗，開倉賑稟三十餘郡。

translations.literal: ' On the gengyin day, envoys were sent separately to go among poor commoners, report truth on floating and redundant persons, open granaries and relieve with grain in more than thirty commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 April 93 CE, envoys fanned out to register the poor and vagrants truthfully and opened granaries for relief in over thirty commanderies.

sentences.zh: 夏四月壬子，封阜陵王种兄魴為阜陵王。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, renzi day, the elder brother Fang of King Zhong of Fuling was enfeoffed as king of Fuling.
translations.idiomatic: On 29 May 93 CE, Fang, elder brother of the late King Zhong of Fuling, was invested as the new prince of Fuling.

sentences.zh: 六月丁酉，郡國三雨雹。

translations.literal: Sixth month, dingyou day, three commanderies and kingdoms rained hail.
translations.idiomatic: On 14 July 93 CE hailstorms struck three commanderies.

sentences.zh: 秋九月辛酉，廣宗王萬歲薨，無子，國除。

translations.literal: Autumn, ninth month, xinyou day, King Wansui of Guangzong died; having no son, the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 October 93 CE Prince Wansui of Guangzong died without an heir, and his fief was abolished.

sentences.zh: 匈奴單于於除鞬叛，遣中朗將任尚討滅之。

translations.literal: The Xiongnu Chanyu Yuchujian rebelled; General of the Gentlemen of the Household Ren Shang was sent to attack and destroy him.
translations.idiomatic: When Chanyu Yuchujian of the Xiongnu rebelled, Ren Shang, a general of the household, was sent to crush him.

sentences.zh: 壬午，令郡縣勸民蓄蔬食以助五谷。

translations.literal: On the renwu day, it was ordered that commanderies and counties urge the people to store vegetable foods to assist the five grains.
translations.idiomatic: On 16 October 93 CE, counties were told to encourage households to lay in vegetables and greens to supplement the staple grains.

sentences.zh: 其官有陂池，令得采取，勿收假稅二歲。

translations.literal: Where officials have ponds and pools, order that gathering be allowed; do not collect rental tax for two years.
translations.idiomatic: Where government-held ponds and marshes supplied food, the people might gather freely and pay no lease tax for two years.

sentences.zh: 冬十月辛未，太尉尹睦薨。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, xinwei day, Grand Commandant Yin Mu died.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 November 93 CE, Grand Commandant Yin Mu died.

sentences.zh: 十一月乙丑，太僕張酺為太尉。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, yichou day, Grand Coachman Zhang Fu became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 November 93 CE, Zhang Fu rose from Grand Coachman to Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 是歲，武陵郡兵破叛蠻，降之。

translations.literal: That year, Wuling commandery troops defeated the rebel Man and made them surrender.
translations.idiomatic: That year troops of Wuling commandery defeated the rebel Man and forced their submission.

sentences.zh: 護羌校尉貫友討燒當羌，羌乃遁去。

translations.literal: Colonel Who Protects the Qiang Guan You attacked the Shaodang Qiang; the Qiang then fled away.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Guan You, charged with the Qiang frontier, attacked the Shaodang band, who then withdrew.

sentences.zh: 南單于安國叛，骨都侯喜斬之。

translations.literal: The Southern Chanyu Anguo rebelled; the Gutu Marquis Xi beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: When the Southern Chanyu Anguo rebelled, the Gutu marquis Xi killed him.

sentences.zh: 六年春正月，永昌徼外夷遣使譯獻犀牛、大象。

translations.literal: Sixth year, spring, first month, tribes beyond the passes of Yongchang sent envoys with interpreters presenting rhinoceros and elephant.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of spring, 94 CE, peoples beyond Yongchang's frontier sent interpreters with tribute of rhinoceros and elephants.

sentences.zh: 己卯，司徒丁鴻薨。

translations.literal: On the jimao day, Minister of Education Ding Hong died.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 January 94 CE, Minister of Education Ding Hong died.

sentences.zh: 二月乙未，遣謁者分行稟貸三河、兗、冀、青州貧民。

translations.literal: Second month, yiwei day, messengers were sent separately to go distribute grain loans to poor commoners in Sanhe, Yan, Ji, and Qing provinces.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 February 94 CE, imperial messengers toured Sanhe, Yan, Ji, and Qing to lend grain from state granaries to the poor.

sentences.zh: 許侯馬光自殺。

translations.literal: Marquis of Xu Ma Guang committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Guang, Marquis of Xu, took his own life.

sentences.zh: 丁未，司空劉方為司徒，太常張奮為司空。

translations.literal: On the dingwei day, Minister of Works Liu Fang became Minister of Education; Grand Master of Ceremonies Zhang Fen became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 February 94 CE, Liu Fang moved from Minister of Works to Minister of Education, and Zhang Fen from Grand Master of Ceremonies to Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 三月庚寅，詔流民所過郡國皆實稟之，其有販賣者勿出租稅，又欲就賤還歸者，復一歲田租、更賦。

translations.literal: Third month, gengyin day, it was ordered that commanderies and kingdoms through which floating people pass all truly grant them grain; those who peddle and sell are not to pay rent tax; moreover, those who wish to return home where prices are cheap are exempted one year's field rent and substitute levy.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 March 94 CE, an edict required every commandery through which vagrants passed to feed them from granaries, exempted hawkers from market tax, and waived one year's land tax and poll substitute for those returning to cheap home districts.

sentences.zh: 丙寅，詔曰：『朕以眇末，承奉鴻烈。

translations.literal: On the bingyin day an edict said: 'We, by Our slight end, receive and uphold the vast blazing legacy.
translations.idiomatic: On 14 April 94 CE an edict began: I am young and slight, yet I bear the weight of the great enterprise.

sentences.zh: 陰陽不和，水旱違度，濟、河之域，凶饉流亡，而未獲忠言至謀，所以匡救之策。

translations.literal: Yin and yang are not harmonious; flood and drought exceed the norm; in the Ji and Yellow River regions, famine and wandering exile, yet We have not obtained loyal words and supreme plans—means whereby to rescue.
translations.idiomatic: Yin and yang are out of joint; floods and droughts break the seasonal pattern; along the Ji and the Yellow River people starve and flee, yet I have heard no candid counsel—no strategy to set things right.

sentences.zh: 寤寐永歎，用思孔疚。

translations.literal: Waking and sleeping We sigh long; thereby Our thoughts are greatly pained.
translations.idiomatic: I sigh awake and asleep; the ache of it is deep.

sentences.zh: 惟官人不得於上，黎民不安于下，有司不念寬和，而競為苛刻，覆案不急，以妨民事，甚非所以上當天心，下濟元元也。

translations.literal: Only—officials are not obtained aright above; the black-haired people are not secure below; the responsible offices do not think of leniency and harmony, but compete to be harsh and severe; reviewing cases not urgent, thereby obstructing the people's affairs—this is very much not the means to match Heaven's mind above or succor the masses below.
translations.idiomatic: Officials misfit for their posts vex the throne; the people know no peace below. Bureaus chase harshness instead of mercy, reopen trivial suits, and block the people's business—hardly the way to answer Heaven or rescue the common folk.

sentences.zh: 思得忠良之士，以輔朕之不逮。

translations.literal: We wish to obtain loyal and good shi to assist Our insufficiency.
translations.idiomatic: I long for loyal, able men to remedy my shortcomings.

sentences.zh: 其令三公、中二千石、二千石、內郡守相舉賢良方正、能直言極諫之士各一人。

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that the Three Dukes, central two-thousand-dan, two-thousand-dan, and inner commandery governors and chancellors each recommend one worthy and good, upright and correct, able to speak bluntly and remonstrate to the utmost.
translations.idiomatic: Let the Three Dukes, full and regular two-thousand-dan officials, and inner commandery governors each nominate one man noted for integrity and fearless remonstrance.

sentences.zh: 昭巖穴，披幽隱，遣詣公車，朕將悉聽焉。

translations.literal: Illuminate cliff and cave; open the secluded and hidden; send them to the Public Carriage gate; We will hear them all.
translations.idiomatic: Search the recluses in cliff and grove, bring hidden talent to light, and send nominees to the Public Carriage office—I will hear every one.

sentences.zh: 』帝乃親臨策問，選補郎吏。

translations.literal: ' The emperor then personally attended planked questioning and selected and supplemented Gentlemen clerks.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then presided in person over the examination, after which successful candidates were appointed Gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，蜀郡徼外羌率種人遣使內附。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, Qiang beyond the passes of Shu commandery led their tribesmen and sent envoys to attach inward.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer, Qiang beyond Shu commandery's frontier led their clans to submit and sent envoys to court.

sentences.zh: 五月，城陽王淑薨，無子，國除。

translations.literal: Fifth month, King Shu of Chengyang died; having no son, the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Prince Shu of Chengyang died without an heir, and his principality was abolished.

sentences.zh: 六月己酉，初令伏閉盡日。

translations.literal: Sixth month, jiyou day, it was first ordered that the fu closure last the whole day.
translations.idiomatic: On 30 June 94 CE, for the first time the summer "closed" days of the calendar were ordered to run the full length of the day.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，京師旱。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, the capital region suffered drought.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn drought gripped the capital.

sentences.zh: 詔中都官徒各除半刑，讁其未竟，五月已下皆免遣。

translations.literal: It was ordered that capital-office convict laborers each have half their sentence removed; as for those not finished, five months and below—all are released and sent away.
translations.idiomatic: An edict halved the terms of convicts in capital prisons, commuted unfinished sentences, and freed all those with five months or less remaining.

sentences.zh: 丁巳，幸洛陽寺，錄囚徒，舉冤獄。

translations.literal: On the dingsi day, he favored Luoyang prison with his presence, recorded prisoners, and raised unjust imprisonments.
translations.idiomatic: On 7 August 94 CE he visited the Luoyang jail, reviewed cases, and redressed wrongful convictions.

sentences.zh: 收洛陽令下獄抵罪，司隸校尉、河南尹皆左降。

translations.literal: The Luoyang magistrate was seized and cast into prison to answer for his crime; the Metropolitan Superintendent and Governor of Henan were both degraded leftward.
translations.idiomatic: The magistrate of Luoyang was jailed for his crimes, while the Metropolitan Superintendent and the governor of Henan were demoted.

sentences.zh: 未及還宮而澍雨。

translations.literal: Before he had returned to the palace, soaking rain fell.
translations.idiomatic: Before he reached the palace again, a soaking rain began to fall.

sentences.zh: 西域都護班超大破焉耆、尉犁，斬其王。

translations.literal: Protector-General of the Western Regions Ban Chao greatly defeated Yanqi and Yuli and beheaded their kings.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Chao, Protector-General of the Western Regions, crushed Yanqi and Yuli and executed their kings.

sentences.zh: 自是西域降服，納質者五十餘國。

translations.literal: From this the Western Regions submitted; those sending hostages were more than fifty states.
translations.idiomatic: After that more than fifty Western Regions states submitted and sent hostages.

sentences.zh: 南單于安國從弟子逢侯率叛胡亡出塞。

translations.literal: The Southern Chanyu Anguo's cousin's son Feng Hou led rebel Hu and fled out beyond the passes.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Hou, a nephew of the Southern Chanyu Anguo, led rebel tribesmen out through the frontier.

sentences.zh: 九月癸丑，以光祿勳鄧鴻行車騎將軍事，與越騎校尉馮柱、行度遼將軍朱徽、使匈奴中郎將杜崇討之。

translations.literal: Ninth month, guichou day, Household Counselor Deng Hong was put in charge of the affairs of General of Chariots and Cavalry, together with Colonel of the Agile Cavalry Feng Zhu, Acting General Who Crosses the Liao Zhu Hui, and General of the Gentlemen of the Household to the Xiongnu Du Chong, to punish him.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 October 94 CE, Deng Hong, Household Counselor, was given acting command of the chariots and cavalry, with Feng Zhu, Zhu Hui acting as General Who Crosses the Liao, and Du Chong, envoy to the Xiongnu, to campaign against Feng Hou.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，護烏桓校尉任尚率烏桓、鮮卑，大破逢侯，馮柱遣兵追擊，復破之。

translations.literal: Winter, eleventh month, Colonel Who Protects the Wuhuan Ren Shang led Wuhuan and Xianbi and greatly defeated Feng Hou; Feng Zhu sent troops in pursuit and struck again, defeating him.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month of winter Colonel Ren Shang, charged with the Wuhuan, led Wuhuan and Xianbi allies in a crushing defeat of Feng Hou; Feng Zhu sent reinforcements in pursuit and broke him a second time.

sentences.zh: 詔以勃海郡屬冀州。

translations.literal: An edict ordered that Bohai commandery be attached to Ji province.
translations.idiomatic: By edict Bohai commandery was transferred to Ji province.

sentences.zh: 武陵漊中蠻叛，郡兵討平之。

translations.literal: The Man of Wuling Louzhong rebelled; commandery troops attacked and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: The Man of Louzhong in Wuling rose in revolt; county troops suppressed them.

sentences.zh: 七年春正月，行車騎將軍鄧鴻、度遼將軍朱徽、中郎將杜崇皆下獄死。

translations.literal: Seventh year, spring, first month, Acting General of Chariots and Cavalry Deng Hong, General Who Crosses the Liao Zhu Hui, and General of the Gentlemen of the Household Du Chong were all cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of spring, 95 CE, Deng Hong, acting general of chariots and cavalry, Zhu Hui, General Who Crosses the Liao, and Du Chong, general of the household, were imprisoned and died.

sentences.zh: 夏四月辛亥朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, xinhai new-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse occurred at the new moon of the fourth month, 18 May 95 CE.

sentences.zh: 帝引見公卿問得失，令將、大夫、御史、謁者、博士、議郎、郎官會廷中，各言封事。

translations.literal: The emperor led in and met dukes and ministers, asking gain and loss; he ordered generals, grandees, imperial clerks, messengers, erudits, advisory gentlemen, and gentlemen of the palace to assemble in the court, each speaking sealed memorials.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor summoned the high ministers to discuss policy, and ordered generals, senior officials, imperial clerks, messengers, academicians, advisory gentlemen, and palace gentlemen to gather in the courtyard and submit sealed memorials.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『元首不明，化流無良，政失於民，讁見於天。

translations.literal: An edict said: 'The head is not enlightened; transformation flows without the good; government misses among the people; reproof appears in Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: The edict continued: when the sovereign lacks clarity, bad customs spread, government fails the people, and Heaven shows its rebuke.

sentences.zh: 深惟庶事，五教在寬，是以舊典因孝廉之舉，以求其人。

translations.literal: Deeply pondering the multitude of affairs—the five teachings rest in leniency—therefore the old statutes rely on the raising of filial and incorrupt to seek the right men.
translations.idiomatic: I have weighed every duty: the five teachings depend on leniency, and the statutes therefore use the filial-and-incorrupt nominations to find fit men.

sentences.zh: 有司詳選郎官寬博有謀、才任典城者三十人。

translations.literal: Let the responsible offices in detail select thirty gentlemen of the palace who are broad, learned, and have strategy, whose talent fits governing a walled city.
translations.idiomatic: Let the bureaus carefully choose thirty Gentlemen who are learned, broad-minded, and fit to govern a county or a marquisate.

sentences.zh: 』既而悉以所選郎出補長、相。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon all the selected gentlemen were sent out to fill magistrate and chancellor posts.
translations.idiomatic: Those thirty were then all posted as county magistrates or chancellors of marquisates.

sentences.zh: 五月辛卯，改千乘國為樂安國。

translations.literal: Fifth month, xinmao day, the state of Qiansheng was changed to the state of Le'an.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 June 95 CE, the principality of Qiansheng was renamed Le'an.

sentences.zh: 六月丙寅，沛王定薨。

translations.literal: Sixth month, bingyin day, King Ding of Pei died.
translations.idiomatic: On 30 June 95 CE, King Ding of Pei died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月乙巳，易陽地裂。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, yisi day, the ground split at Yiyang.
translations.idiomatic: On 18 August 95 CE the earth gaped open at Yiyang.

sentences.zh: 九月癸卯，京師地震。

translations.literal: Ninth month, guimao day, the capital region had an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On 15 October 95 CE an earthquake struck the capital region.

sentences.zh: 八年春二月己丑，立貴人陰氏為皇后。

translations.literal: Eighth year, spring, second month, jichou day, Honored Lady Yin was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 March 96 CE, Lady Yin the Honored Lady was elevated to empress.

sentences.zh: 賜天下男子爵，人二級，三老、孝悌、力田三級，民無名數及流民欲占者一級；

translations.literal: The empire's males were granted noble ranks, each two grades; the three elders, filial and fraternal, and strength-in-farming, three grades; commoners without registered numbers and floating people who wished to register, one grade;
translations.idiomatic: Every male in the realm received two steps of honorary rank; the three elders, exemplars of filial piety and fraternal duty, and outstanding farmers received three; unregistered persons and vagrants who took residence received one;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃，貧不能自存者粟，人五斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the seriously ill and crippled, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain, five hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the gravely ill, and the destitute received five hu of grain each.

sentences.zh: 南匈奴右溫禺犢王叛，為寇。

translations.literal: The Southern Xiongnu king of the Right Wenyu rebelled and became a marauder.
translations.idiomatic: The Southern Xiongnu Prince of the Right Wenyu rebelled and raided the frontier.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，行度遼將軍龐奮、越騎校尉馮柱追討之，斬右溫禺犢王。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, Acting General Who Crosses the Liao Pang Fen and Colonel of the Agile Cavalry Feng Zhu pursued and attacked him, beheading the king of the Right Wenyu.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn Pang Fen, acting General Who Crosses the Liao, and Feng Zhu, colonel of agile cavalry, ran him down and beheaded the Prince of the Right Wenyu.

sentences.zh: 車師後王叛，擊其前王。

translations.literal: The Rear Jushi king rebelled and attacked his Front king.
translations.idiomatic: The king of Rear Jushi rebelled and attacked the king of Front Jushi.

sentences.zh: 八月辛酉，飲酎。

translations.literal: Eighth month, xinyou day, the zhòu libation was drunk.
translations.idiomatic: On 13 September 96 CE the court performed the autumn zhoul offering at the ancestral temple.

sentences.zh: 詔郡國中都官繫囚減死一等，詣敦煌戍。

translations.literal: It was ordered that in commanderies and kingdoms capital-office imprisoned convicts have death reduced one grade and go to Dunhuang for garrison duty.
translations.idiomatic: An edict commuted capital cases among convicts in central-government jails by one degree and sent them to frontier service at Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 其犯大逆，募下蠶室；

translations.literal: Those who committed great rebellion are to be summoned down to the silkworm chamber;
translations.idiomatic: Those guilty of great treason were castrated in the silkworm chamber;

sentences.zh: 其女子宮。

translations.literal: the women, palace punishment.
translations.idiomatic: women convicted with them suffered confinement in the palace workshops.

sentences.zh: 自死罪已下，至司寇及亡命者入贖，各有差。

translations.literal: From death crime downward, down to Sikou and fugitives with warrant, entry ransom, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: Lesser crimes down through penal servitude and fugitives could commute punishment with graded ransom payments.

sentences.zh: 九月，京師蝗。

translations.literal: Ninth month, the capital region had locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts swarmed the capital in the ninth month.

sentences.zh: 吏民言事者，多歸責有司。

translations.literal: Among clerks and commoners who spoke on affairs, many returned blame to the responsible offices.
translations.idiomatic: Officials and commoners who memorialized mostly blamed the bureaucracy.

sentences.zh: 詔曰：『蝗蟲之異，殆不虛生，萬方有罪，在予一人，而言事者專咎自下，非助我者也。

translations.literal: An edict said: 'The prodigy of locusts probably is not born empty; the myriad regions have guilt—it rests in Us one man; yet those who speak on affairs solely blame those below—they are not those who assist Us.
translations.idiomatic: The edict went on: locusts do not appear without cause. If the realm has fault, it lies with me alone; memorials that heap blame only on subordinates do not help me.

sentences.zh: 朕寤寐恫矜，思弭憂釁。

translations.literal: We, waking and sleeping, are pained and pitying; We think to quell worry and misfortune.
translations.idiomatic: I lie awake grieving, seeking how to end this calamity.

sentences.zh: 昔楚嚴無灾而懼，成王出郊而反風。

translations.literal: In the past King Zhuang of Chu had no calamity yet feared; King Cheng went out to the suburbs and the wind reversed.
translations.idiomatic: King Zhuang of Chu feared Heaven though no disaster had struck; King Cheng of Zhou left the city and the ill wind turned aside.

sentences.zh: 將何以匡朕不逮，以塞灾變？

translations.literal: With what shall they correct Our insufficiency, thereby to stop disaster and prodigy?
translations.idiomatic: What will you do to remedy my failings and avert these omens?

sentences.zh: 百僚師尹，勉修厥職，刺史、二千石詳刑辟，理冤虐，恤鰥寡，矜孤弱，思惟致灾興蝗之咎。

translations.literal: Hundred officials and master governors, strive to cultivate your offices; regional inspectors and two-thousand-dan, examine punishments and law, order unjust cruelty, pity widowers and widows, pity orphans and weak—think on the fault that brings disaster and raises locusts.
translations.idiomatic: Let every minister and prefect strive in his duty; let regional inspectors and governors review penal cases, right wrongful cruelty, pity the widowed and orphaned, and ponder what fault has brought Heaven to send locusts.

sentences.zh: 』庚子，復置廣陽郡。

translations.literal: ' On the gengzi day, Guangyang commandery was re-established.
translations.idiomatic: On 14 September 96 CE, day gengzi, Guangyang commandery was restored.

sentences.zh: 九年春正月，永昌徼外蠻夷及撣國重譯奉貢。

translations.literal: Ninth year, spring, first month, Man and Yi beyond the passes of Yongchang and the state of Shan, through double translation, presented tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of spring, 97 CE, tribes beyond Yongchang and the kingdom of Shan, through relay interpreters, sent tribute to court.

sentences.zh: 三月庚辰，隴西地震。

translations.literal: Third month, gengchen day, Longxi had an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 April 97 CE, Longxi commandery was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 癸巳，濟南王康薨。

translations.literal: On the guisi day, King Kang of Jinan died.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 May 97 CE, King Kang of Jinan died.

sentences.zh: 西域長史王林擊車師後王，斬之。

translations.literal: Chief Clerk of the Western Regions Wang Lin attacked the Rear Jushi king and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Lin, Chief Clerk of the Western Regions, attacked the king of Rear Jushi and executed him.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丁卯，封樂成王黨子巡為樂成王。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, dingmao day, the son Xun of King Dang of Lecheng was enfeoffed as king of Lecheng.
translations.idiomatic: On 25 May 97 CE, Xun, son of Prince Dang of Lecheng, succeeded him as prince of Lecheng.

sentences.zh: 六月，蝗、旱。

translations.literal: Sixth month, there were locusts and drought.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month locusts and drought struck.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，詔：『今年秋稼為蝗蟲所傷，皆勿收租、更、芻稾；

translations.literal: On the wuchen day an edict: 'This year's autumn crops harmed by locusts—all do not collect rent, substitute levy, or fodder straw;
translations.idiomatic: On 26 June 97 CE an edict waived field rent, the substitute levy, and fodder tax on all crops damaged by locusts.

sentences.zh: 若有所損失，以實除之，餘當收租者亦半入。

translations.literal: if there is some loss, remove according to the actual amount; for the remainder that ought to collect rent, also half payment.
translations.idiomatic: Where damage was partial, remissions matched actual loss; where tax was still due, only half was collected.

sentences.zh: 其山林饒利，陂池漁采，以贍元元，勿收假稅。』

translations.literal: As for mountains and forests with abundant profit, ponds and pools for fishing and gathering—to supply the masses—do not collect rental tax.'
translations.idiomatic: Gathering and fishing in rich woodlands and government ponds was left tax-free to help feed the people.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，蝗蟲飛過京師。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, locusts flew past the capital.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of autumn a swarm of locusts passed over the capital.

sentences.zh: 八月，鮮卑寇肥如，遼東太守祭參下獄死。

translations.literal: Eighth month, the Xianbi raided Feiru; Governor of Liaodong Zai Shen was cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month the Xianbi attacked Feiru; Zai Shen, governor of Liaodong, was jailed and died there.

sentences.zh: 閏月辛巳，皇太后竇氏崩。

translations.literal: Intercalary month, xinsi day, Empress Dowager Dou died.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 October 97 CE, intercalary eighth month, day xinsi, Empress Dowager Dou died.

sentences.zh: 丙申，葬章德皇后。

translations.literal: On the bingshen day, Empress Zhangde was buried.
translations.idiomatic: On 10 October 97 CE she was buried with the posthumous title Empress Zhangde.

sentences.zh: 燒當羌寇隴西，殺長吏，遣行征西將軍劉尚、越騎校尉趙世等討破之。

translations.literal: The Shaodang Qiang raided Longxi and killed senior clerks; Acting General Who Conquers the West Liu Shang, Colonel of the Agile Cavalry Zhao Shi, and others were sent to attack and defeat them.
translations.idiomatic: The Shaodang Qiang raided Longxi and killed local officials; Liu Shang, acting general for the western campaign, Zhao Shi, colonel of agile cavalry, and others defeated them.

sentences.zh: 九月庚申，司徒劉方策免，自殺。

translations.literal: Ninth month, gengshen day, Minister of Education Liu Fang was dismissed by rescript and committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 October 97 CE, Minister of Education Liu Fang was stripped of office by edict and took his own life.

sentences.zh: 甲子，追尊皇妣梁貴人為皇太后。

translations.literal: On the jiazi day, the emperor's late mother Honored Lady Liang was posthumously honored as empress dowager.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 October 97 CE the emperor's birth mother, Lady Liang the Honored Lady, was posthumously titled empress dowager.

sentences.zh: 冬十月乙酉，改葬恭懷梁皇后于西陵。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, yiyou day, Empress Gonghuai nee Liang was reburied at Western Tumulus.
translations.idiomatic: On 16 November 97 CE Empress Liang, posthumously called Gonghuai, was reinterred at the Western mausoleum park.

sentences.zh: 十一月癸卯，光祿勳河南呂蓋為司徒。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, guimao day, Household Counselor Lü Gai of Henan became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 December 97 CE Lü Gai of Henan, Household Counselor, was appointed Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 十二月丙寅，司空張奮罷。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, bingyin day, Minister of Works Zhang Fen was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 31 December 97 CE Minister of Works Zhang Fen was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 壬申，太僕韓稜為司空。

translations.literal: On the renshen day, Grand Coachman Han Ling became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 6 January 98 CE Han Ling rose from Grand Coachman to Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 己丑，復置若廬獄官。

translations.literal: On the jichou day, the Ruolu prison office was re-established.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 January 98 CE the Ruolu prison warden's post was restored.

sentences.zh: 十年春三月壬戌，詔曰：『堤防溝渠，所以順助地理，通利壅塞。

translations.literal: Tenth year, spring, third month, renxu day, an edict said: 'Dikes and defenses, ditches and canals are the means to comply with and assist earth patterns, to penetrate benefit and clear blockage.
translations.idiomatic: On 30 March 98 CE an edict declared that dikes and irrigation works exist to work with the land and keep water moving.

sentences.zh: 今廢慢懈弛，不以為負。

translations.literal: Now they are abandoned, slow, slack, and relaxed; it is not taken as a burden.
translations.idiomatic: Yet they have been neglected and slackened, and no one treats dereliction as a fault.

sentences.zh: 刺史、二千石其隨宜疏導。

translations.literal: Regional inspectors and two-thousand-dan, according to suitability, dredge and lead.
translations.idiomatic: Let regional inspectors and governors clear and channel them as local conditions require.

sentences.zh: 勿因緣妄發，以為煩擾，將顯行其罰。』

translations.literal: Do not on pretext rashly mobilize, thereby making vexatious disturbance—We will clearly carry out their punishment.'
translations.idiomatic: They must not launch pointless corvées that harass the people; offenders will be punished openly.

sentences.zh: 夏五月，京師大水。

translations.literal: Summer, fifth month, the capital region had great flood.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month of summer the capital was hit by serious flooding.

sentences.zh: 秋七月己巳，司空韓稜薨。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, jisi day, Minister of Works Han Ling died.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 August 98 CE, Minister of Works Han Ling died.

sentences.zh: 八月丙子，太常太山巢堪為司空。

translations.literal: Eighth month, bingzi day, Grand Master of Ceremonies Chao Kan of Taishan became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 August 98 CE Chao Kan of Taishan, Grand Master of Ceremonies, became Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 九月庚戌，復置廩犧官。

translations.literal: Ninth month, gengxu day, the granary and sacrifice officials were re-established.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 October 98 CE the granary and sacrificial-victim offices were restored.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，五州雨水。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, five provinces had rain and water.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month of winter five provinces suffered heavy rain and flooding.

sentences.zh: 十二月，燒當羌豪迷唐等率種人詣闕貢獻。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, Shaodang Qiang chieftains Mitang and others led their tribesmen to the palace gate to present tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month the Shaodang Qiang leader Mitang and his followers came to the capital with tribute.

sentences.zh: 戊寅，梁王暢薨。

translations.literal: On the wuyin day, King Chang of Liang died.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 January 99 CE, King Chang of Liang died.

sentences.zh: 十一年春二月，遣使循行郡國，稟貸被灾害不能自存者，令得漁采山林池澤，不收假稅。

translations.literal: Eleventh year, spring, second month, envoys were sent to tour commanderies and kingdoms, granting grain loans to those harmed by disaster who could not support themselves, ordering that they be allowed to fish and gather in mountains, forests, ponds, and marshes without collecting rental tax.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of spring, 99 CE, inspectors toured the empire, lending grain to disaster victims and allowing them to use public woods and waters without lease tax.

sentences.zh: 丙午，詔郡國中都官徒及篤癃老小女徒各除半刑，其未竟三月者，皆免歸田里。

translations.literal: On the bingwu day it was ordered that in commanderies and kingdoms capital-office convict laborers and seriously ill, old, small, and female convict laborers each have half their sentence removed; those not finished with three months—all are released to return to fields and hamlets.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 March 99 CE an edict halved the terms of capital convicts who were aged, infirm, juvenile, or female, and freed anyone with three months or less left to serve.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙寅，大赦天下。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, bingyin day, a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On 20 April 99 CE a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 己巳，復置右校尉官。

translations.literal: On the jisi day, the Colonel of the Right post was re-established.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 April 99 CE the office of Colonel of the Right was restored.

sentences.zh: 秋七月辛卯，詔曰：『吏民逾僭，厚死傷生，是以舊令節之制度。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, xinmao day, an edict said: 'Clerks and commoners exceed and usurp; they thicken death and injure life—therefore the old ordinances restrain them with regulations.
translations.idiomatic: On 6 August 99 CE an edict complained that officials and commoners broke sumptuary law, lavishing wealth on the dead at the living's expense, which older statutes had tried to curb.

sentences.zh: 頃者貴戚近親，百僚師尹，莫肯率從，有司不舉，怠放日甚。

translations.literal: Recently honored kin and near relatives, the hundred officials and master governors, none willing to lead and follow; the responsible offices do not report; negligence and license grow daily worse.
translations.idiomatic: Lately even imperial in-laws and high ministers refused to obey, while censors looked the other way and laxity grew worse by the day.

sentences.zh: 又商賈小民，或忘法禁，奇巧靡貨，流積公行。

translations.literal: Moreover merchants and petty commoners sometimes forget law prohibitions; odd and clever extravagant goods flow and pile, openly practiced.
translations.idiomatic: Traders and humble folk ignored the bans on luxury goods, which flooded the market openly.

sentences.zh: 其在位犯者，當先舉正。

translations.literal: Those among office-holders who offend ought first to be reported and corrected.
translations.idiomatic: Officials who violated these rules were to be prosecuted first.

sentences.zh: 市道小民，但且申明憲綱，勿因科令，加虐羸弱。』

translations.literal: Marketway petty commoners—for the time being only clarify the law net; do not rely on statutes and orders to add cruelty to the weak and emaciated.'
translations.idiomatic: Ordinary townspeople were to be warned under the law, not crushed by harsh enforcement against the frail.

sentences.zh: 十二年春二月，旄牛徼外白狼、貗薄夷率種人內屬。

translations.literal: Twelfth year, spring, second month, beyond Maoniu the White Wolf and Hubo Yi beyond the passes led their tribesmen to attach inward.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of spring, 100 CE, the White Wolf and Hubo tribes beyond Maoniu led their people to submit to Han.

sentences.zh: 詔貸被灾諸郡民種糧。

translations.literal: It was ordered to lend seed grain to the people of the various commanderies that suffered disaster.
translations.idiomatic: An edict lent seed grain to farmers in disaster-struck commanderies.

sentences.zh: 賜下貧、鰥、寡、孤、獨、不能自存者，及郡國流民，聽入陂池漁采，以助蔬食。

translations.literal: To the very poor, widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, those unable to support themselves, and floating people of commanderies and kingdoms, permission was given to enter ponds and pools to fish and gather, to assist vegetable food.
translations.idiomatic: The destitute, widowed, orphaned, and vagrants were allowed to fish and forage in public ponds to supplement their diet.

sentences.zh: 三月丙申，詔曰：『比年不登，百姓虛匱。
