translations.literal: Third month, bingshen day, an edict said: 'In recent years the harvest has not risen; the hundred surnames are empty and exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 April 100 CE an edict began: harvests have failed in recent years and the people are drained.

sentences.zh: 京師去冬無宿雪，今春無澍雨，黎民流離，困於道路。

translations.literal: The capital last winter had no lingering snow; this spring has no soaking rain; the black-haired people drift and scatter, trapped on the roads.
translations.idiomatic: Last winter brought no lasting snow to the capital; this spring no soaking rain; the people wander homeless and choke the highways.

sentences.zh: 朕痛心疾首，靡知所濟。

translations.literal: We are pained to the heart and ache in the head; We do not know by what to cross over.
translations.idiomatic: My heart aches and my temples throb; I see no way through.

sentences.zh: 「瞻仰昊天，何辜今人？

translations.literal: "Gazing up at vast Heaven, what guilt have the people of today?
translations.idiomatic: As the ode asks, I look up to vast Heaven: what sin have these people committed?

sentences.zh: 」三公，朕之腹心，而未獲承天安民之策。

translations.literal: " The Three Dukes are Our belly and heart, yet We have not obtained the strategy to receive Heaven and pacify the people.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Dukes are my closest counselors, yet I still have no plan to answer Heaven and give the people peace.

sentences.zh: 數詔有司，務擇良吏。

translations.literal: Repeatedly We have ordered the responsible offices to strive to select good clerks.
translations.idiomatic: Again and again I have ordered the bureaus to appoint honest magistrates.

sentences.zh: 今猶不改，競為苛暴，侵愁小民，以求虛名，委任下吏，假勢行邪。

translations.literal: Now still they do not reform; they compete to be harsh and violent; they encroach and vex the petty commoners in order to seek empty reputation; they entrust lower clerks who borrow power to practice wickedness.
translations.idiomatic: Nothing has changed: men still compete in cruelty, harry the poor for empty reputation, and leave junior clerks to abuse delegated power.

sentences.zh: 是以令下而蕊生，禁至而詐起。

translations.literal: Therefore when orders go down, calyxes sprout; when prohibitions arrive, deceit arises.
translations.idiomatic: So each new decree breeds fresh abuse, and every tightening of the ban brings new fraud.

sentences.zh: 巧法析律，飾文增辭，貨行於言，罪成乎手，朕甚病焉。

translations.literal: Clever at law, splitting statutes, adorning text and increasing phrases, goods move in words, guilt is completed in the hand—We are greatly sick of it.
translations.idiomatic: They twist the code, dress up writs, take bribes in speech, and seal guilt with a stroke—I am sick of it.

sentences.zh: 公卿不思助明好惡，將何以救其咎罰？

translations.literal: The dukes and ministers do not think to assist in clarifying good and evil—with what shall they rescue their fault and punishment?
translations.idiomatic: If you ministers will not help me distinguish right from wrong, how can you escape blame when punishment falls?

sentences.zh: 咎罰既至，復令灾及小民。

translations.literal: When fault and punishment have arrived, again they cause disaster to reach the petty commoners.
translations.idiomatic: When retribution comes, it is always the common people who suffer the disaster.

sentences.zh: 若上下同心，庶或有廖。

translations.literal: If above and below share one heart, perhaps there may be some cure.
translations.idiomatic: Only if court and countryside pull together is there any hope of relief.

sentences.zh: 其賜天下男子爵，人二級，三老、孝悌、力田三級，民無名數及流民欲占者人一級；

translations.literal: Let there be bestowed on the empire's males noble ranks, each two grades; the three elders, filial and fraternal, and strength-in-farming, three grades; commoners without registered numbers and floating people who wish to register, one grade each;
translations.idiomatic: Let every male in the realm receive two steps of honorary rank; the three elders and models of filial piety and farming, three steps; unregistered persons and vagrants who settle, one step;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人三斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the seriously ill and crippled, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain, three hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the gravely ill, and the destitute shall have three hu of grain each.

sentences.zh: 』壬子，賜博士員弟子在太學者布，人三匹。

translations.literal: ' On the renzi day, to erudits' quota disciples who are in the Imperial Academy, cloth was bestowed, three bolts per person.
translations.idiomatic: On 6 May 100 CE, students enrolled under the academicians at the Imperial Academy received three bolts of cloth each.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，日南象林蠻夷反，郡兵討破之。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, the Man and Yi of Xianglin in Rinan rebelled; commandery troops attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer the tribes of Xianglin in Rinan commandery rebelled; local troops defeated them.

sentences.zh: 閏月，賑貸敦煌、張掖、五原民下貧者糓。

translations.literal: Intercalary month, grain was lent to relieve the very poor among the people of Dunhuang, Zhangye, and Wuyuan.
translations.idiomatic: In the intercalary month the court lent grain to the poorest households in Dunhuang, Zhangye, and Wuyuan.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，秭歸山崩。

translations.literal: On the wuchen day, a mountain at Zigui collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 June 100 CE a landslide struck Mount Zigui.

sentences.zh: 六月，舞陽大水，賜被水灾尤貧者穀，人三斛。

translations.literal: Sixth month, Wuyang had great flood; to those harmed by water disaster who were especially poor, grain was bestowed, three hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Wuyang was inundated; the neediest flood victims received three hu of grain each.

sentences.zh: 秋七月辛亥朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, xinhai new-moon day, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse marked the new moon of the seventh month, 16 August 100 CE.

sentences.zh: 九月戊午，太尉張酺免。

translations.literal: Ninth month, wuwu day, Grand Commandant Zhang Fu was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 October 100 CE Grand Commandant Zhang Fu was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 丙寅，大司農張禹為太尉。

translations.literal: On the bingyin day, Grand Minister of Agriculture Zhang Yu became Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On 28 October 100 CE Zhang Yu moved from Grand Minister of Agriculture to Grand Commandant.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，西域蒙奇、兜勒二國遣使內附，賜其王金印紫綬。

translations.literal: Winter, eleventh month, the two states of Mengqi and Doule of the Western Regions sent envoys to attach inward; their kings were granted gold seals and purple ribbons.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month of winter the Western Regions states Mengqi and Doule sent envoys to submit; their kings received gold seals on purple cords.

sentences.zh: 是歲，燒當羌復叛。

translations.literal: That year, the Shaodang Qiang again rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: That year the Shaodang Qiang rose again in revolt.

sentences.zh: 十三年春正月丁丑，帝幸東觀，覽書林，閱篇籍，博選術蓺之士以充其官。

translations.literal: Thirteenth year, spring, first month, dingchou day, the emperor favored the Eastern View with his presence, perused the forest of books, reviewed documents, and broadly selected men of arts and skills to fill its offices.
translations.idiomatic: On 12 February 101 CE the emperor visited the Eastern Institute, browsed the library, and recruited skilled scholars to staff it.

sentences.zh: 二月，任城王尚薨。

translations.literal: Second month, King Shang of Rencheng died.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month King Shang of Rencheng died.

sentences.zh: 丙午，賑貸張掖、居延、朔方、日南貧民及孤、寡、羸弱不能自存者。

translations.literal: On the bingwu day, relief loans were given to poor commoners of Zhangye, Juyan, Shuofang, and Rinan, and to orphans, widowers, widows, the weak and emaciated unable to support themselves.
translations.idiomatic: On 19 March 101 CE the court lent relief grain to the poor of Zhangye, Juyan, Shuofang, and Rinan, including orphans, widows, and the feeble.

sentences.zh: 秋八月，詔象林民失農桑業者，賑貸種糧，稟賜下貧穀食。

translations.literal: Autumn, eighth month, it was ordered that Xianglin commoners who had lost farming and mulberry work be lent seed grain and granted grain food to the very poor.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month of autumn an edict lent seed to Xianglin farmers who had lost their crops and gave grain to the destitute there.

sentences.zh: 己亥，北宮盛饌門閣火。

translations.literal: On the jihai day, the gate-tower of the Lavish Viands gate of the Northern Palace caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On 13 September 101 CE fire broke out in the gatehouse of the Lavish Viands gate at the Northern Palace.

sentences.zh: 護羌校尉周鮪擊燒當羌，破之，荊州雨水。

translations.literal: Colonel Who Protects the Qiang Zhou Wei attacked the Shaodang Qiang and defeated them; Jing province had rain and water.
translations.idiomatic: Colonel Zhou Wei defeated the Shaodang Qiang, while Jing province suffered heavy rains and flooding.

sentences.zh: 九月壬子，詔曰：『荊州比歲不節，今茲淫水為害，餘雖頗登，而多不均浹，深惟四民農食之本，慘然懷矜。

translations.literal: Ninth month, renzi day, an edict said: 'Jing province in recent years has lacked moderation; now this year excessive water does harm; though the rest is somewhat abundant, much is not evenly pervasive; We deeply ponder the four people's root in farming and food, and mournfully cherish pity.
translations.idiomatic: On 26 September 101 CE an edict noted that Jing province's weather had been erratic for years and this year's floods had done harm; other regions had fared unevenly, and the ruler grieved for the people's livelihood.

sentences.zh: 其今天下半入今年田租、芻稾；

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that all under Heaven pay half this year's field rent and fodder straw;
translations.idiomatic: Let the empire pay only half this year's land tax and fodder levy;

sentences.zh: 有宜以實除者，如故事。

translations.literal: where it is fitting to remove according to the actual amount, as in former practice.
translations.idiomatic: where damage warrants it, remit by the usual rules according to actual loss.

sentences.zh: 貧民假種食，皆勿收責。』

translations.literal: Poor commoners' borrowed seed and food—all do not collect repayment.'
translations.idiomatic: Do not call in seed or food loans made to the poor.

sentences.zh: 冬十一月，安息國遣使獻師子及條枝大爵。

translations.literal: Winter, eleventh month, the state of Anxi sent envoys presenting a lion and large birds from Tiaozhi.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month of winter Parthia sent envoys with a lion and great ostriches from the west.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，詔曰：『幽、幷、涼州戶口率少，邊役眾劇，束脩良吏，進仕路狹。

translations.literal: On the bingchen day an edict said: 'You, Bing, and Liang provinces on average have few households and mouths; border service is numerous and harsh; for well-groomed good clerks the path of advancement to office is narrow.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 November 101 CE an edict noted that You, Bing, and Liang provinces were thinly populated yet bore heavy frontier duty, so able men had few routes to office.

sentences.zh: 撫接夷狄，以人為本。

translations.literal: To soothe and receive Yi and Di takes people as root.
translations.idiomatic: Pacifying the border tribes depends on having the right men in place.

sentences.zh: 其令緣邊郡口十萬以上，歲舉孝廉一人；

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that frontier commanderies with mouths above one hundred thousand, each year recommend one filial and incorrupt;
translations.idiomatic: Frontier commanderies of more than one hundred thousand persons may nominate one filial-and-incorrupt candidate each year;

sentences.zh: 不滿十萬，二歲舉一人；

translations.literal: those not reaching one hundred thousand, in two years recommend one;
translations.idiomatic: those below that figure, one every two years;

sentences.zh: 五萬以下，三歲舉一人。

translations.literal: those below fifty thousand, in three years recommend one.
translations.idiomatic: those under fifty thousand, one every three years.

sentences.zh: 』鮮卑寇右北平，遂入漁陽，漁陽太守擊破之。

translations.literal: ' The Xianbi raided Youbeiping, then entered Yuyang; the governor of Yuyang attacked and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbi raided Youbeiping and pushed into Yuyang, where the governor drove them off.

sentences.zh: 戊辰，司徒呂蓋罷。

translations.literal: On the wuchen day, Minister of Education Lü Gai was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 December 101 CE Minister of Education Lü Gai was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，遣使者督荊州兵討巫蠻，破降之。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, envoys were sent to supervise Jing province troops in punishing the Wu Man; they were defeated and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer, 102 CE, imperial envoys directed Jing troops against the Wu Man, broke them, and accepted their surrender.

sentences.zh: 庚辰，賑貸張掖、居延、敦煌、五原、漢陽、會稽流民下貧穀，各有差。

translations.literal: On the gengchen day, grain relief loans were given to floating people and the very poor of Zhangye, Juyan, Dunhuang, Wuyuan, Hanyang, and Kuaiji, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: On 15 May 102 CE the court distributed graded grain relief to vagrants and the destitute in Zhangye, Juyan, Dunhuang, Wuyuan, Hanyang, and Kuaiji.

sentences.zh: 五月丁未，初置象林將兵長史官。

translations.literal: Fifth month, dingwei day, the office of Chief Clerk with Troops for Xianglin was first established.
translations.idiomatic: On 11 June 102 CE the court created the post of chief clerk with military authority at Xianglin.

sentences.zh: 六月辛卯，廢皇后陰氏，后父特進綱自殺。

translations.literal: Sixth month, xinmao day, Empress nee Yin was deposed; her father Specially Advanced Gang committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: On 25 June 102 CE Empress Yin was deposed; her father Yin Gang, holder of the Specially Advanced rank, killed himself.

sentences.zh: 秋七月甲寅，詔復象林縣更賦、田租、芻稾二歲。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, jiayin day, it was ordered to restore to Xianglin county the substitute levy, field rent, and fodder straw for two years.
translations.idiomatic: On 6 August 102 CE an edict remitted for two years the poll substitute, land tax, and fodder levy for Xianglin county.

sentences.zh: 壬子，常山王側薨。

translations.literal: On the renzi day, King Ce of Changshan died.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 September 102 CE, King Ce of Changshan died.

sentences.zh: 是秋，三州雨水。

translations.literal: That autumn, three provinces had rain and water.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn three provinces were inundated by rain.

sentences.zh: 冬十月甲申，詔：『兗、豫、荊州今年水雨淫過，多傷農功。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, jiashen day, an edict: 'Yan, Yu, and Jing provinces this year have had excessive rain and water, greatly harming agricultural work.
translations.idiomatic: On 8 November 102 CE an edict noted excessive rain in Yan, Yu, and Jing that had ruined the harvest.

sentences.zh: 其令被害什四以上皆半入田租、芻稾；

translations.literal: Let it be ordered that where harm reaches forty percent or more, all pay half field rent and fodder straw;
translations.idiomatic: Where crops lost forty percent or more, land tax and fodder levy were halved;

sentences.zh: 其不滿者，以實除之。

translations.literal: where it does not reach that, remove according to the actual amount.
translations.idiomatic: lesser damage was remitted in proportion to actual loss.

sentences.zh: 』辛卯，立貴人鄧氏為皇后。

translations.literal: ' On the xinmao day, Honored Lady nee Deng was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: On 15 November 102 CE Lady Deng the Honored Lady was made empress.

sentences.zh: 丁酉，司空巢堪罷。

translations.literal: On the dingyou day, Minister of Works Chao Kan was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 November 102 CE Minister of Works Chao Kan was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 十一月癸卯，大司農徐防為司空。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, guimao day, Grand Minister of Agriculture Xu Fang became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 5 December 102 CE Xu Fang rose from Grand Minister of Agriculture to Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 是歲，初復郡國上計補郎官。

translations.literal: That year, it was first restored that commanderies and kingdoms' year-end accounting reports supplement Gentlemen of the Palace.
translations.idiomatic: That year, 103 CE, the court for the first time again appointed Gentlemen from candidates presented in the commanderies' annual accounting to the capital.

sentences.zh: 十五年春閏月乙未，詔流民欲還歸本而無糧食者，過所實稟之，疾病加致醫藥；

translations.literal: Fifteenth year, spring, intercalary month, yiwei day, it was ordered that floating people who wished to return home but lacked grain and food—at each pass let them truly be granted grain; if ill, in addition provide medicine;
translations.idiomatic: On 24 March 103 CE, in the intercalary second month, an edict ordered waystations to feed vagrants returning home without supplies and to give medicine to the sick among them;

sentences.zh: 其不欲還歸者，勿強。

translations.literal: those who do not wish to return—do not force.
translations.idiomatic: those who did not wish to go home were not to be compelled.

sentences.zh: 二月，詔稟貸潁川、汝南、陳留、江夏、梁國、敦煌貧民。

translations.literal: Second month, it was ordered to grant grain loans to poor commoners of Yingchuan, Runan, Chenliu, Jiangxia, Liang state, and Dunhuang.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month the court ordered grain loans for the poor in Yingchuan, Runan, Chenliu, Jiangxia, Liang, and Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲子晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, last day jiazi, there was an eclipse of the sun.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse fell on the last day of the fourth month, 21 May 103 CE.

sentences.zh: 五月戊寅，南陽大風。

translations.literal: Fifth month, wuyin day, Nanyang had great wind.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 June 103 CE a violent windstorm struck Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 六月，詔令百姓鰥、寡漁采陂池，勿收假稅二歲。

translations.literal: Sixth month, it was ordered that commoners who were widowers or widows fish and gather in ponds and pools; do not collect rental tax for two years.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month an edict allowed widowers and widows to fish and forage in public waters without lease tax for two years.

sentences.zh: 秋七月丙寅，濟南王錯薨。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, bingyin day, King Cuo of Jinan died.
translations.idiomatic: On 31 July 103 CE, King Cuo of Jinan died.

sentences.zh: 復置涿郡故安鐵官。

translations.literal: The iron office of Gu'an in Zhuo commandery was re-established.
translations.idiomatic: The state ironworks at Gu'an in Zhuo commandery was restored.

sentences.zh: 九月壬午，南巡狩，清河王慶、濟北王壽、河間王開並從。

translations.literal: Ninth month, renwu day, southern hunt; Kings Qing, Shou, and Kai of Qinghe, Jibei, and Hejian all followed.
translations.idiomatic: On 27 September 103 CE the emperor set out on a southern tour; Princes Qing of Qinghe, Shou of Jibei, and Kai of Hejian accompanied him.

sentences.zh: 賜所過二千石長吏以下、三老、官屬及民百年者錢、布，各有差。

translations.literal: Along the route of passage, to two-thousand-dan senior clerks and below, the three elders, official subordinates, and commoners a hundred years old, cash and cloth were bestowed, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: Along his route he gave graded gifts of cash and cloth to local officials, elders, staffs, and any commoner who had reached a hundred years.

sentences.zh: 是秋，四州雨水。

translations.literal: That autumn, four provinces had rain and water.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn four provinces were flooded by rain.

sentences.zh: 冬十月戊申，幸章陵，祠舊宅。

translations.literal: Winter, tenth month, wushen day, he favored Zhangling with his presence and sacrificed at the old residence.
translations.idiomatic: On 2 November 103 CE he visited Zhangling and offered sacrifice at the old Liu family home.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，祠園廟，會宗室於舊廬，勞賜作樂。

translations.literal: On the guichou day, he sacrificed at the park temple, assembled the imperial clan at the old hut, rewarded and bestowed, and had music performed.
translations.idiomatic: On 7 November he sacrificed at the tombs, feasted the imperial kin at the ancestral dwelling, gave gifts, and had music played.

sentences.zh: 戊午，進幸雲夢，臨漢水而還。

translations.literal: On the wuwu day, he advanced and favored Yunmeng with his presence, approached the Han River, and returned.
translations.idiomatic: On 12 November he proceeded to Yunmeng, viewed the Han River, and turned back.

sentences.zh: 十一月甲申，車駕還宮，賜從臣及留者公卿以下錢、布，各有差。

translations.literal: Eleventh month, jiashen day, the carriage returned to the palace; to followers and those who remained, from dukes and ministers down, cash and cloth were bestowed, each according to gradation.
translations.idiomatic: On 20 November the court returned to the palace; followers and officials who had stayed behind received graded gifts of cash and cloth.

sentences.zh: 十二月庚子，琅邪王宇薨。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, gengzi day, King Yu of Langye died.
translations.idiomatic: On 15 December 103 CE, King Yu of Langye died.

sentences.zh: 有司奏，以為夏至則微陰起，靡草死，可以決小事。

translations.literal: The responsible offices submitted a memorial, holding that at the summer solstice then slight yin arises, the spreading grass dies, and one may decide minor matters.
translations.idiomatic: Officials memorialized that at the summer solstice, when yin first stirs and soft herbs wither, minor legal cases might be settled.

sentences.zh: 是歲，初令郡國以日北至案薄刑。

translations.literal: That year, it was first ordered that commanderies and kingdoms on the day of the sun's northern utmost review light punishments.
translations.idiomatic: That year the court for the first time ordered commanderies to review light sentences on the winter solstice.

sentences.zh: 十六年春正月己卯，詔貧民有田業而以匱乏不能自農者，貸種糧。

translations.literal: Sixteenth year, spring, first month, jimao day, it was ordered that poor commoners who had field holdings but through want could not farm themselves be lent seed grain.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 February 104 CE an edict lent seed grain to poor farmers who owned land but lacked means to work it.

sentences.zh: 二月己未，詔兗、豫、徐、冀四州比年雨多傷稼，禁沽酒。

translations.literal: Second month, jiwei day, it was ordered that in the four provinces of Yan, Yu, Xu, and Ji, in recent years rain has been much and harmed the crops—prohibit selling wine.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 February 104 CE, because repeated rains had ruined crops in Yan, Yu, Xu, and Ji, the court banned the sale of wine.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，遣三府掾分行四州，貧民無以耕者，為雇犁牛直。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, clerks of the Three Offices were sent separately to tour the four provinces; for poor commoners who had no means to plow, plow-ox hire cost was provided for them.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer the Three Offices sent clerks to the four provinces to pay plow-ox rental for farmers too poor to cultivate.

sentences.zh: 五月壬午，趙王商薨。

translations.literal: Fifth month, renwu day, King Shang of Zhao died.
translations.idiomatic: On 15 May 104 CE, King Shang of Zhao died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，旱。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, drought.
translations.idiomatic: The seventh month of autumn brought drought.

sentences.zh: 戊午，詔曰：『今秋稼方穗而旱，雲雨不霑，疑吏行慘刻，不宣恩澤，妄拘無罪，幽閉良善所致。

translations.literal: On the wuwu day an edict said: 'Now the autumn crops are just in ear yet there is drought; clouds and rain do not moisten—We suspect it is caused by clerks practicing cruel harshness, not spreading kindly moisture, rashly detaining the guiltless, and imprisoning the good.
translations.idiomatic: On 29 July 104 CE an edict blamed drought on cruel officials who spread no mercy, jailed the innocent, and wronged the good.

sentences.zh: 其一切囚徒於法疑者勿決，以奉秋令。

translations.literal: Let all imprisoned convicts whose cases are doubtful in law not be decided, thereby to observe the autumn ordinance.
translations.idiomatic: All doubtful cases were to be left undecided in observance of the autumn rule of leniency.

sentences.zh: 方察煩苛之吏，顯明其罰。

translations.literal: We shall investigate vexatious and harsh clerks and clearly manifest their punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Harsh and vexatious officials were to be investigated and punished openly.

sentences.zh: 』辛酉，司徒魯恭免。

translations.literal: ' On the xinyou day, Minister of Education Lu Gong was removed.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 August 104 CE Minister of Education Lu Gong was dismissed.

sentences.zh: 庚午，光祿勳張酺為司徒。

translations.literal: On the gengwu day, Household Counselor Zhang Fu became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On 10 August 104 CE Zhang Fu, Household Counselor, became Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，詔令天下皆半入今年田租、芻稾；

translations.literal: On the xinsi day, it was ordered that all under Heaven pay half this year's field rent and fodder straw;
translations.idiomatic: On 21 August 104 CE the empire was ordered to pay only half the year's land tax and fodder levy;

sentences.zh: 其被灾害者，以實除之。

translations.literal: those struck by disaster, remove according to the actual amount.
translations.idiomatic: disaster areas were to receive remissions matching actual loss.

sentences.zh: 貧民受貸種糧及田租、芻稾，皆勿收責。

translations.literal: Poor commoners who received lent seed grain and field rent and fodder straw—all do not collect repayment.
translations.idiomatic: The poor were not to be asked to repay seed loans or remitted taxes.

sentences.zh: 十二月，復置遼東西部都尉官。

translations.literal: Twelfth month, the western commandant post of Liaodong was re-established.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month the western commandant office for Liaodong was restored.

sentences.zh: 元興元年春正月戊午，引三署郎召見禁中，選除七十五人，補謁者、長、相。

translations.literal: First year of Yuanxing, spring, first month, wuwu day, he led in the gentlemen of the three bureaus and summoned them to audience within the forbidden precincts; seventy-five were selected and appointed, to fill messenger, magistrate, and chancellor posts.
translations.idiomatic: On 22 February 105 CE the emperor interviewed Gentlemen of the three bureaus in the palace, selected seventy-five, and appointed them as messengers, magistrates, or chancellors.

sentences.zh: 高句驪寇郡界。

translations.literal: Gaogouli raided the commandery border.
translations.idiomatic: Koguryŏ raided the frontier commanderies.

sentences.zh: 夏四月庚午，大赦天下，改元元興。

translations.literal: Summer, fourth month, gengwu day, a general amnesty was proclaimed for all under Heaven; the era name was changed to Yuanxing.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 May 105 CE a general amnesty was proclaimed and the era name changed to Yuanxing.

sentences.zh: 宗室以罪絕者，悉復屬籍。

translations.literal: Among the imperial clan those cut off by crime—all had their clan registers restored.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial kinsmen who had been struck from the rolls for crimes were restored to clan status.

sentences.zh: 五月癸酉，雍地裂。

translations.literal: Fifth month, guiyou day, the Yong region's ground split.
translations.idiomatic: On 7 June 105 CE the earth opened in the Yong capital region.

sentences.zh: 秋九月，遼東太守耿夔擊貊人，破之。

translations.literal: Autumn, ninth month, Governor of Liaodong Geng Kui attacked the Mo people and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month of autumn Geng Kui, governor of Liaodong, defeated the Mo tribesmen.

sentences.zh: 冬十二月辛未，帝崩於章德前殿，年二十七。

translations.literal: Winter, twelfth month, xinwei day, the emperor collapsed at Zhangde Front Hall; he was twenty-seven sui.
translations.idiomatic: On 13 February 106 CE the emperor died in the Zhangde Front Hall at the age of twenty-seven.

sentences.zh: 立皇子隆為皇太子。

translations.literal: The imperial son Long was established as heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's son Liu Long was named crown prince.

sentences.zh: 賜天下男子爵，人二級，三老、孝悌、力田人三級，民無名數及流民欲占者人一級；

translations.literal: The empire's males were granted noble ranks, each two grades; the three elders, filial and fraternal, and strength-in-farming, three grades per person; commoners without registered numbers and floating people who wish to register, one grade per person;
translations.idiomatic: Every male received two steps of honorary rank; the three elders and models of filial piety and farming received three; unregistered persons and vagrants who settled received one;

sentences.zh: 鰥、寡、孤、獨、篤癃、貧不能自存者粟，人三斛。

translations.literal: to widowers, widows, orphans, solitaries, the seriously ill and crippled, and the poor unable to support themselves, grain, three hu per person.
translations.idiomatic: widowers, widows, orphans, the childless, the gravely ill, and the destitute received three hu of grain each.

sentences.zh: 自竇憲誅後，帝躬親萬機。

translations.literal: From the time Dou Xian was executed, the emperor personally attended to the myriad handles of state.
translations.idiomatic: After Dou Xian's execution the emperor took personal charge of government.

sentences.zh: 每有灾異，輒延問公卿，極言得失。

translations.literal: Whenever there were disasters and prodigies, he always extended inquiry to dukes and ministers, letting them speak to the utmost on gain and loss.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever omens appeared he called in the high ministers and urged them to speak freely on policy.

sentences.zh: 前後符瑞八十一所，自稱德薄，皆抑而不宣。

translations.literal: Before and after there were eighty-one places with talismanic auspices; calling his own virtue thin, he all suppressed and did not proclaim them.
translations.idiomatic: Eighty-one auspicious signs were reported during his reign, but he deemed his virtue slight and ordered none of them publicized.

sentences.zh: 舊南海獻龍眼、荔支，十里一置，五里一候，奔騰阻險，死者繼路。

translations.literal: Formerly Nanhai presented longan and lychee: every ten li a relay station, every five li a post; galloping across perilous defiles, the dead followed one another on the road.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly the south sent longan and lychee north under a relay system that killed horses and drivers on the dangerous roads.

sentences.zh: 時臨武長汝南唐羌，縣接南海，乃上書陳狀。

translations.literal: At that time the magistrate of Linwu, Tang Qiang of Runan, whose county bordered Nanhai, then submitted a memorial stating the situation.
translations.idiomatic: Tang Qiang of Runan, magistrate of Linwu on the Nanhai border, then memorialized describing the harm.

sentences.zh: 帝下詔曰：『遠國珍羞，本以薦奉宗廟，茍有傷害，豈愛民之本。

translations.literal: The emperor issued an edict saying: 'Distant states' rare delicacies were originally to present and serve the ancestral temple; if there is harm, how is that the root of loving the people?
translations.idiomatic: The emperor replied: exotic delicacies from afar were meant for the ancestral shrines; if they harm the people, that cannot be what loving the people means.

sentences.zh: 其勑太官勿復受獻。

translations.literal: Let the Grand Provisioner be ordered not again to accept presentations.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the Grand Provisioner to accept no more such tribute.

sentences.zh: 』由是遂省焉。

translations.literal: ' From this it was then reduced.
translations.idiomatic: The practice was thereafter discontinued.

sentences.zh: 論曰：自中興以後，逮於永元，雖頗有弛張，而俱存不擾，是以齊民歲增，闢土世廣。

translations.literal: The appraisal says: From the restoration onward, down to the Yongyuan era, though there was some loosening and tightening, yet all preserved non-disturbance; therefore the orderly people increased year by year, and opened land broadened generation by generation.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: from Guangwu's restoration through the Yongyuan years, policy tightened and loosened, yet the state avoided needless meddling; the population grew each year and frontier land steadily widened.

sentences.zh: 偏師出塞，則漠北地空；

translations.literal: When a partial army went out beyond the passes, then the desert north's land was emptied;
translations.idiomatic: A single column beyond the wall could empty the northern desert of Xiongnu power;

sentences.zh: 都護西指，則通譯四萬。

translations.literal: when the Protector-General pointed west, then interpreters reached forty thousand li.
translations.idiomatic: when the Protector-General turned west, translation and tribute reached forty thousand li.

sentences.zh: 豈其道遠三代，術長前世？

translations.literal: Is it that their way surpassed the Three Dynasties, their methods exceeded prior ages?
translations.idiomatic: Was Han policy truly wiser than the Three Dynasties', its methods better than earlier ages?

sentences.zh: 將服叛去來，自有數也？

translations.literal: Or is it that submission and rebellion, going and coming, have their own reckoning?
translations.idiomatic: Or did submission and rebellion simply follow their own pattern, as fate would have it?

sentences.zh: 孝殤皇帝諱隆，和帝少子也。

translations.literal: The Filial and Early-Departed Emperor, whose taboo name was Long, was the younger son of Emperor He.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Shang, whose personal name was Long, was the younger son of Emperor He.

sentences.zh: 元興元年十二月辛未夜，即皇帝位，時誕育百餘日。

translations.literal: First year of Yuanxing, twelfth month, xinwei night, he assumed the imperial throne; at the time of birth and nurture he was a hundred-odd days.
translations.idiomatic: On the night of 13 February 106 CE he ascended the throne while still only a little over three months old.

sentences.zh: 尊皇后曰皇太后，太后臨朝。

translations.literal: The empress was honored as empress dowager; the empress dowager presided over court from behind the screen.
translations.idiomatic: The empress became empress dowager and ruled as regent.

sentences.zh: 北匈奴遣使稱臣，詣敦煌奉獻。

translations.literal: The Northern Xiongnu sent envoys declaring themselves subjects, going to Dunhuang to present tribute.
translations.idiomatic: The Northern Xiongnu sent envoys offering submission and tribute at Dunhuang.

sentences.zh: 延平元年春正月辛卯，太尉張禹為太傅，司徒徐防為太尉，參錄尚書事，百官總己以聽。

translations.literal: First year of Yanping, spring, first month, xinmao day, Grand Commandant Zhang Yu became Grand Tutor; Minister of Education Xu Fang became Grand Commandant, jointly put in charge of the Masters of Writing; the hundred officials gathered themselves and obeyed.
translations.idiomatic: On 18 February 106 CE Zhang Yu moved from Grand Commandant to Grand Tutor; Xu Fang from Minister of Education to Grand Commandant with shared authority over the Masters of Writing; all officials answered to them.

sentences.zh: 封皇兄勝為平原王。

translations.literal: The emperor's elder brother Sheng was enfeoffed as king of Pingyuan.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor's elder brother Liu Sheng was made prince of Pingyuan.

sentences.zh: 癸卯，光祿勳梁鮪為司徒。

translations.literal: On the guimao day, Household Counselor Liang Wei became Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On the guimao day Liang Wei, Household Counselor, was appointed Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 三月甲申，葬孝和皇帝於慎陵，尊廟曰穆宗。

translations.literal: Third month, jiashen day, the Filial and Harmonious Emperor He was buried at Shen Mausoleum; the temple was honored as Muzong.
translations.idiomatic: On 1 April 106 CE Emperor He was buried at the Shen mausoleum with the temple name Muzong.

sentences.zh: 丙戌，清河王慶、濟北王壽、河間王開、常山王章始就國。

translations.literal: On the bingxu day, Kings Qing, Shou, Kai, and Zhang of Qinghe, Jibei, Hejian, and Changshan for the first time went to their states.
translations.idiomatic: On 3 April 106 CE Princes Qing of Qinghe, Shou of Jibei, Kai of Hejian, and Zhang of Changshan departed for their fiefs.

sentences.zh: 五月辛卯，皇太后詔曰：『皇帝幼沖，承統鴻業，朕且權佐助聽政，兢兢寅畏，不知所濟。

translations.literal: Fifth month, xinmao day, the empress dowager issued an edict saying: 'The emperor is young and tender; he receives the lineage of the vast enterprise; We for the time being shall assist in listening to government with temporary authority, fearful and reverent, not knowing by what to cross over.
translations.idiomatic: On 8 June 106 CE the empress dowager proclaimed: the emperor is an infant bearing the great mandate; I shall assist in governing for the time being, in fear and trembling, unsure how to proceed.

sentences.zh: 深惟至治之本，道化在前，刑罰在後。

translations.literal: We deeply ponder the root of utmost good order: the way and transformation come first, punishments come after.
translations.idiomatic: I reflect that perfect order rests on moral instruction first and punishments only after.

sentences.zh: 將稽中和，廣施慶惠，與吏民更始。

translations.literal: We shall examine centrality and harmony, broadly apply felicitous grace, and with clerks and commoners make a fresh beginning.
translations.idiomatic: I shall seek even-tempered rule, spread blessing widely, and give officials and people a new start.

sentences.zh: 其大赦天下。

translations.literal: Let there be a general amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Let there be a general amnesty throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 自建武以來諸犯禁錮，詔書雖解，有司持重，多不奉行，其皆復為平民。

translations.literal: From Jianwu onward all who violated proscription—though edicts released them, the responsible offices held heavy and mostly did not carry them out—let them all again become commoners.
translations.idiomatic: All persons proscribed since Guangwu's reign, though earlier edicts had freed them yet bureaus had often not complied, are now fully restored as commoners.

sentences.zh: 』壬辰，河東垣山崩。

translations.literal: ' On the renchen day, a mountain at Yuan in Hedong collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: On 9 June 106 CE a mountain collapsed at Yuan in Hedong.

sentences.zh: 六月丁未，太常尹勤為司空。

translations.literal: Sixth month, dingwei day, Grand Master of Ceremonies Yin Qin became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 July 106 CE Yin Qin, Grand Master of Ceremonies, was appointed Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 郡國三十七雨水。

translations.literal: Thirty-seven commanderies and kingdoms had rain and water.
translations.idiomatic: Thirty-seven commanderies and kingdoms suffered flooding.

sentences.zh: 己未，詔曰：『自夏以來，陰雨過節，煗氣不效，將有厥咎。

translations.literal: On the jiwei day an edict said: 'From summer onward, yin rain has exceeded the norm; warm qi has not taken effect—there will be its fault.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 August 106 CE an edict noted that since summer unseasonable rains had continued and warmth had failed—surely an omen of fault.

sentences.zh: 寤寐憂惶，未知所由。

translations.literal: Waking and sleeping We are anxious and frightened, not knowing the cause.
translations.idiomatic: I lie awake in anxiety, not knowing the cause.

sentences.zh: 昔夏后惡衣服，菲飲食，孔子曰「吾無閒然」。

translations.literal: In the past the lord of Xia disliked fine clothes and meagered food and drink; Confucius said, "I have no fault to find in that."
translations.idiomatic: The founder of Xia wore coarse clothes and ate plain food, and Confucius said he could find no fault in him.

sentences.zh: 今新遭大憂，且歲節未和，徹膳損服，庶有補焉。

translations.literal: Now newly having suffered great mourning, and moreover the year's seasons are not harmonious, remove delicacies and reduce garments—perhaps there will be remedy.
translations.idiomatic: We have just entered deep mourning and the seasons are still awry; let me cut palace fare and wardrobe in hope of amendment.

sentences.zh: 其減太官、導官、尚方、內署諸服御珍膳靡麗難成之物。』

translations.literal: Let there be reduction in the Grand Provisioner, Conductor of Viands, Imperial Workshop, and inner offices of all garments, viands, rare delicacies, extravagant splendor, and hard-to-produce goods.'
translations.idiomatic: Reduce the Grand Provisioner, the viands office, the Imperial Workshop, and inner bureaus in costly clothing and extravagant foods that tax the people to produce.

sentences.zh: 丁卯，詔司徒、大司農、長樂少府曰：『朕以無德，佐助統政，夙夜經營，懼失厥衷。

translations.literal: On the dingmao day, it was ordered to the Minister of Education, Grand Minister of Agriculture, and Superintendent of Changle: 'We lack virtue yet assist in ordering government; day and night We manage affairs, fearing to miss the center.
translations.idiomatic: On 12 August 106 CE the empress dowager ordered the Minister of Education, Grand Minister of Agriculture, and Superintendent of Changle: I lack virtue yet help govern; night and day I labor, fearing to miss the right course.

sentences.zh: 思惟治道，由近及遠，先內後外。

translations.literal: We ponder the way of good order: from near reaching far, first inner then outer.
translations.idiomatic: Good order begins at court and spreads outward; the inner must be set right before the outer.

sentences.zh: 自建武之初以至于今，八十餘年，宮人歲增，房御彌廣。

translations.literal: From the beginning of Jianwu down to now, more than eighty years, palace women have increased year by year, bedchamber attendants ever broader.
translations.idiomatic: For more than eighty years since Jianwu the number of palace women has grown each year and the imperial household ever larger.

sentences.zh: 又宗室坐事沒入者，猶托名公族，甚可愍焉。

translations.literal: Moreover those of the imperial clan who by affairs sat and were confiscated inward still borrow the name of ducal clan—very pitiable.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial clansmen enslaved for crimes still bear the label of the Liu house—most pitiable.

sentences.zh: 今悉免遣，及掖庭宮人，皆為庶民，以抒幽隔鬱滯之情。

translations.literal: Now let all be released and sent away, and Yeeting palace women—all become commoners, thereby to vent the feelings of secluded separation and pent stagnation.
translations.idiomatic: Release them all, and the women of the harem as well, to commoner status, to ease long pent-up grief in seclusion.

sentences.zh: 諸官府、郡國、王侯家奴婢姓劉及疲癃羸老，皆上其名，務令實悉。』

translations.literal: In all government offices, commanderies and kingdoms, and kings' and marquises' households, slave girls and servants surnamed Liu and the worn, crippled, emaciated, and old—all report their names, striving to make them true and complete.'
translations.idiomatic: Every office, commandery, and princely household must register Liu-surname bondsmen and the aged and infirm, with full and accurate lists.

sentences.zh: 秋七月庚寅，勑司隸校尉、部刺史曰：『夫天降災戾，應政而至。

translations.literal: Autumn, seventh month, gengyin day, the Metropolitan Superintendent and department regional inspectors were charged: 'When Heaven sends down disaster and affliction, it arrives in response to government.
translations.idiomatic: On 4 September 106 CE the Metropolitan Superintendent and regional inspectors were told: Heaven sends calamities in response to how the realm is governed.

sentences.zh: 間者郡國或有水灾，妨害秋稼。

translations.literal: Recently commanderies and kingdoms sometimes had water disaster, harming and obstructing the autumn crops.
translations.idiomatic: Lately some commanderies have flooded and ruined the autumn harvest.

sentences.zh: 朝廷惟咎，憂惶悼懼。

translations.literal: The court ponders fault, anxious, frightened, mourning, and fearful.
translations.idiomatic: The court blames itself and grieves in fear.

sentences.zh: 而郡國欲獲豐穰虛飾之譽，遂覆蔽灾害，多張墾田，不揣流亡，競增戶口，掩匿盜賊，令姦惡無懲，署用非次，選舉乖宜，貪苛慘毒，延及平民。

translations.literal: Yet commanderies and kingdoms wish to obtain the empty reputation of abundant harvest, then cover and hide disaster harm, greatly inflate reclaimed fields, do not measure floating exile, compete to increase household and mouth counts, conceal and hide bandits and thieves, causing the wicked to go unpunished; appointments are not by order, selection and raising miss what is fitting; greedy harshness cruel and poisonous extends to the orderly commoners.
translations.idiomatic: Yet counties seek false credit for bumper crops: they hide flood damage, inflate reclaimed acreage, ignore refugees while padding household rolls, conceal banditry so villains go unpunished, make irregular appointments, botch nominations, and let greedy cruelty reach the common people.

sentences.zh: 刺史垂頭塞耳，阿私下比，「不畏於天，不愧於人」。

translations.literal: Regional inspectors hang their heads and stop their ears, flatter private and compare below—"they do not fear Heaven, they are not ashamed before others."
translations.idiomatic: Inspectors hang their heads and plug their ears, curry favor below, and show neither fear of Heaven nor shame before men.

sentences.zh: 假貸之恩，不可數恃，自今以後，將糾其罰。

translations.literal: The grace of lent loans cannot be repeatedly relied on; from now onward, We shall investigate their punishment.
translations.idiomatic: You cannot count forever on imperial leniency; from now on I will punish such abuses.

sentences.zh: 二千石長吏其各實覈所傷害，為除田租，芻稾。』

translations.literal: Two-thousand-dan senior clerks, let each truly verify the harm suffered and for them remove field rent and fodder straw.'
translations.idiomatic: Let every governor verify real damage and remit land tax and fodder accordingly.

sentences.zh: 八月辛亥，帝崩。

translations.literal: Eighth month, xinhai day, the emperor collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: On 21 September 106 CE the emperor died.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，殯於崇德前殿。

translations.literal: On the guichou day, he was laid out in the Chongde Front Hall.
translations.idiomatic: On 23 September his coffin rested in the Chongde Front Hall.

sentences.zh: 年二歲。

translations.literal: Age two sui.
translations.idiomatic: He was two sui old.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：『孝和沈烈，率由前則。

translations.literal: The encomium says: 'The Filial and Harmonious was deep and fierce; he followed the prior norms.
translations.idiomatic: The summation praises Emperor He as grave and resolute, following the models of his forebears.

sentences.zh: 王赫自中，賜命彊慝。

translations.literal: The king's awesomeness came from within; he gave mandate to chastise the mighty wicked.
translations.idiomatic: Royal majesty rose from within; he gave command to subdue the powerful and the wicked.

sentences.zh: 抑沒祥符，登顯時德。

translations.literal: He suppressed and submerged auspicious tokens; he raised and displayed timely virtue.
translations.idiomatic: He hid away auspicious omens while displaying the true virtue of the age.

sentences.zh: 殤世何早，平原弗克。』

translations.literal: The Shang emperor's generation—how early; Pingyuan could not overcome.'
translations.idiomatic: Alas that the Shang emperor's reign was cut so short, and Pingyuan could not save him.
